post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 217
Newbie Game 86

Story
A gaggle of explorers,
Hats and torches right at hand,
Journeyed to the land of Egypt,
Also called the land of sand,
Where the men drool over treasures
Cuz the food is way too bland.
It’s probably quite dangerous,
And also very grand.

But sinister forces are plotting,
And they’re plotting very hard,
Planning nasty big surprises
That will no doubt leave you scarred.
Our explorers must be vigilant,
And smart, and be on guard.
But will they be up to task?
Or will they end up very charred?

- Game Rules -
This tab is a summary of the most important rules in this game.

If you are an experienced player: you can skip everything other than this tab and the setup tab.
If you are new to the game: it is recommended that you at least skim through everything. The most important rules are in the three tabs under Game Rules and the Conduct Guidelines tab. Welcome to Mafia!
  1. Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  2. Each "day" phase in this game is 2 weeks in real life while "night" lasts 3 days.
  3. Do not post here during night.
  4. You must format your votes correctly. If a vote wasn't counted, it was probably formatted incorrectly. Neither divergent capitalizations of usernames nor capital i/lowercase L are differentiated; manipulations of either still count as valid votes.
  5. Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  6. Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO.
  7. Votes are locked at LYLO.
  8. Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, QT's, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator QT will be granted upon request.
  9. This game uses Dawn start; Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules, speaking of which, if you've read all the rules below and are ready to start, say something with at least three words that rhyme.
  10. All times will be given in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4), as applicable.
  11. Activity rules for this game are 48 hours to in-game prod and 72 hours to PM prod.
  12. Use common sense.
  13. All other rules are standard. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.
  14. This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. Please PM the mods to request access.
  15. Best of luck, and have fun!

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are the standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PM's from the mods, including role PM's.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this thread to anyone other than the game moderators, unless you are specifically given permission to do so.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent], among others.
    • Hide tags are allowed as long as they are visible.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are dead, do not post in this thread, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and you will be granted access to the spectator QT.
    • Do not impersonate the mods, such as by spoofing votecounts or EOD posts.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be taken into account during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications. Additional details can be found in the Moderation Policies tab.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, hangs, and No Hangs.

    • Vote in bold font in this format: Vote: [player username]. A vote will not be counted if it does not follow this exact format.
    • The capitalization of the player name does not matter, but the exact spelling does. Do not use nicknames.
    • The one exception to the above rule is the use of I (capital i) or l (lowercase L). They are considered identical for the purposes of voting.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes. However, vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjayy., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are not valid votes.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • You can remove a previously made vote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before casting a new vote.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. If you want to purposely fakevote, please try to notify the mods in your role PM so that it will not be accidentally counted.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EoD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately.
    • Upon reaching LYLO, (Hang-or-Lose), deadline hang will be turned off. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • You may also choose on any given day to not hang anyone and No Hang instead by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • If an even number of players are alive, half of the players voting for No Hang will be considered a majority. This only applies to No Hang.
    • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted, even if the moderators have not yet made a post declaring the end of day. If this happens, the game will proceed into twilight.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the end of day, the person hanged will be officially dead, and their role will be revealed. Night will then begin immediately.

  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times and deadlines will be given in the time currently observed on the U.S. East Coast, namely Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will last 336 hours (14 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of day at their discretion, usually in cases of acute inactivity or replacements too close to the deadline.
    • Each night phase will last 72 hours (3 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of night at their discretion.
    • Exact deadlines will be specified at the start of each phase.
    • In determining whether votes cast near the deadline are valid, the moderators will reference the time-of-posting given by the AoPS system. Votes logged as being cast at or after the specified EOD time will not be valid. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • During the night phase, players with unsubmitted, available NA's (night actions) will be sent a PM 36 to 24 hours before the deadline, reminding them to submit their actions.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player - or their partner(s) if applicable - by the night phase deadline, the player will not perform an NA that night. NA submissions made after the night deadline has passed will not be valid.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence). Please post V/LAs in bold in-thread or in your role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LA's should be less than a week long. For longer absences, please contact the moderators for more options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request a replacement by posting the following in-thread or in their role PM: Request Replacement.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours (4.5 days) and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • For details on what constitutes a "game-advancing post," please refer to the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If a player receives 5 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player receives a postban from the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. For more details, please go to the Moderation Policies tab.
- Conduct Guidelines -
This tab is a summary of the tabs following this one, concerning concepts vital to healthy gameplay.

Important note: If you ever find that this game is stressing you out or causing you frustration, please know that, as the moderators, we are here for you. We know that not being able to talk to other people about this game due to OOT rules can feel suffocating at times, so please feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can talk and listen, or even help you find a better way to cope, such as by taking a break from the game. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM as well.
  1. Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  2. Discussions must stay in this thread. Attaching files or linking to external documents concerning this game is not allowed.
  3. Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.
  4. Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  5. You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  6. First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.

  • Sportsmanship
    This tab covers concepts that are conducive to healthy gameplay and the steps moderators may take to intervene if things go wrong.

    Most players will not encounter the situations detailed below and in most cases will not be warned even if they do not observe these guidelines.

    This section will be updated periodically. If this happens while a game is in progress, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP.

    • Do not gamethrow.

    This includes: hard claiming as a mafia for no reason, outing your teammates, posting screenshots of the Mafia QT, hanging a conftown as town
    This does not include: giving up, strategic suiciding, lolcatting, or self-voting

    You should play towards your wincondition(s) to the best of your ability. Intentionally sabotaging the game for any reason - such as to troll, out of a personal vendetta, or out of frustration - is prohibited. Moderator action will differ based on context, but will likely be severe, ranging from a force modkill to a forum ban. Please note that only intentionally playing against your wincon and hurting your teammates is banned - you are otherwise allowed to play however you like, and examples such as giving up, being unhelpful, or making mistakes in good faith will not prompt intervention.

    Important note: According to decisions made by forum moderators detailed in this post, anything that appears to be gamethrowing - whether real or fake - is not allowed. Any serious statement along the lines of "I am actually mafia and/or my partner is [player]" that appears to be made with the intent to gamethrow will result in an immediate modkill, even if the statement is false. As this subject requires nuanced knowledge of the difference between lolcatting, gamethrowing, and the like, we recommend avoiding this type of situation entirely until you have enough experience to navigate the fine line between the two.

    • Publicly discussing player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other moderator interactions is discouraged.

    This includes: "[player] was probably force replaced for trying to PM their mafia partner."
    This does not include: discussing lurking or player activity in reference to their alignment

    Seriously discussing a player's alignment based off of their V/LAs, replacements, warnings, or other essential moderator communications is highly discouraged. Speculation, jokes, or hot takes about such events are okay, but if a serious discussion occurs in the thread, the moderators may step in and ask participants to stop. Repeatedly refusing to listen to the moderators may result in a warning.

    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels are prohibited.

    This includes: an encrypted file only certain players possess the key to, the use of cryptosystems, using encryption programs to hide messages in posts
    This does not include: leaving breadcrumbs, manually encoded/decoded ciphers, messages in the form of a public riddle, brief conversation in non-English languages

    Unless private day-communication is specifically enabled in the game, you are prohibited from attempting to communicate with another player/other players privately. This is an extension of the rule banning posts with concealed text: all game-related information should be visible and reasonably available to be accessed and/or analyzed by all players. If you do wish to establish a secret method of daytime communication with your teammates, it may be a good idea to run your idea by the moderator team, but even if you do not, you will likely only get a warning or receive a request to cease using the method in question.

    • Avoiding referencing OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or creating OGI situations that could be construed as alignment indicative is recommended.

    This includes: adding all game players to a quote-on-quote "unrelated" PM, "[player] is really active in their other games, but not this one. Are they lurking as maf?" or "[player] and [player] have been acting unusually friendly in [this unrelated forum], I bet they're actually the scumteam."
    This does not include: mentioning real life, online interactions, and other unrelated comments as long as they are alignment agnostic

    OGI (Out of Game Information) is exactly what it stands for - anything that happens outside of the game thread - and it is only problematic when it is used alignment-indicatively. The most important things to remember are these: a) do not publicly discuss a read based on OGI, and b) try to avoid situations outside the game thread that could cause other players to suspect your alignment, town or mafia. Everything else is fine, and mentioning things that happened outside the thread or in real life in the game thread is also fine, but it must be alignment agnostic, or reasonably unable to be used to determine your alignment. The purpose of this is to preserve game integrity by minimizing outside influences. The moderators may intervene to stop an OGI discussion or incident and ask participants to cease. Repeated failure to listen to the moderators may result in a warning.

    • Avoiding formatting that makes text hard to read is recommended.

    This includes: bright colors, [size=1], [hide=.], [tip][hide]
    This does not include: colored VCAs, colored responses in quotes, small hide tags used to hide a joke

    As long as a given text is not invisible or effectively so, no warnings will be issued. However, if players complain or if this is abused, the moderators may step in and ask the participant(s) to stop.

  • Etiquette
    This tab covers player etiquette, warning limits, and things players should avoid doing.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not post excessive spam.
    • Other behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Abide by AoPS’s Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense in disputes.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction, depending on the severity. If a player breaks a rule after they have received their maximum number of warnings, or if they commit a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced.

    In addition to these rules, please note the types of posts that are implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are now liable to be warned for these posts. This should have little to no effect on normal player behavior: this enforcement policy has been created to better define the boundaries of the "do not abuse loopholes" rule and to encourage healthier gameplay.

    Discouraged Player Conduct
    This tab provides detailed examples of posts that may result in a warning.

    This section will be updated periodically. If this happens while a game is in progress, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP.

    • Posts with formatting that imitates the text indicating that a post has been edited.
    • Posts that are blank or otherwise contain no content.
    • Unsolicited advertising.
    • Multiple posts with identical content.
    • Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text.
    • Extended and in-depth communication in code or non-English languages.

    Posts in this category typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met:

    1. The player possesses a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior
    2. The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content
    3. The posts have caused notably negative reactions from other players

    Again, the majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines or receive a warning even if they break them. This is simply to better and more transparently enforce the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that are not explicitly forbidden by previous rulesets.

  • Moderation Policies
    This tab provides more detail on how the moderation team will handle notable fringe scenarios.

    • Role Randomization

    When randomizing roles/alignments at the start of a game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. As a result, both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are possible.

    Current RNG Process (Matrix6)
    All randomizations are done by random.org.

    Once the playerlist has been finalized, the setup will be randomized using the Random Integer Generator:



    The numbers correspond to the order in which the 6 possible setups are listed in the Setup tab.

    Once a setup has been chosen, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, Town PRs, Mafia Goon(s), Mafia PR. This list will be randomized using the List Randomizer and overlaid on top of the playerlist, which is ordered first by descending rank and then by alphabetical order. The ordered playerlist of each game can be found in the Alive/Dead tabs.


    • Tiebreaking

    If a two-way tie is reached at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made on Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made on Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This solution was chosen to avoid tiebreaking via RNG while simultaneously preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting. Oh, and by the way, after actually looking through the rules this time and not cheating, PM the moderators the name of a random color and do not leak this rule again.

    The same solution will be applied if a three-way tie or higher is reached at deadline.

    • Votes cast during deadline

    If votes or unvotes are cast before or during the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the official logged post times. These can be found immediately by locating the relevant post at https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/u[account ID of player] and referencing the upper-right corner of the applicable entry.

    Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes shown as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not be counted.

    • Game-advancing Posts

    A "game-advancing post" is literally anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. For consistency purposes, most posts other than those reading "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks.

    • Postbans

    After recent clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, the moderators are no longer allowed to proxy posts from players who have been postbanned. As a result, once a player is postbanned, they will have to be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation, such as if the postban is less than a week in length.

    Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help.

    You may contact the moderators through discord at ashton#1850 or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS.

    • Outside Consulting

    If the moderators have trouble resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity, they may contact a forum mod or other respected moderator for help resolving the incident. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the course of actions the moderators chose, you may request that the moderators consult for a second opinion, if they have not done so already.

  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • mathjay and Socoobo are the moderators of Mafia Game 217.
    • HannahW and Mathletesv contributed to flavor, OCRSTM, feedback, and the management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be given private keys granting access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red-colored text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other official announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • Blue-colored text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green-colored text is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green (#00f752), Activity Check blue (#005dff), mathjay personal blue (#4a86e8), HannahW personal blue (#0b5394), Mathletesv personal blue (#4d94ff), Socoobo personal orange (#ff8000).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days or by the next applicable EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time, but major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OP changelog. The thing you're looking for is this: put a smiley somewhere. Most rule changes will be designed to have as little effect on normal, day-to-day play as possible and will be designed to streamline gameplay.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violations of new rules made before the changes have been announced.
    • Major changes to the OP will be made in between games. The Version History tab will list the most important changes.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make final judgement calls on the moderation of this game.

    MG#217 OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset (OCRSTM) version 4.
    Current version: 4.1.1
    OCRSTM is written and maintained by the Unpaid Intern Moderator Team.
Setup
This game is based on the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
  • Setup 1: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 2: 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 3: 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup A: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup B: 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup C: 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • If applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock in the same night.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.
  • Mafia do not have daychat and may only discuss in the designated Quicktopic during night.

Role PMs
Archaeology Grad Student (Vanilla Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are an Archaeology Grad Student (Vanilla Townie).

Because you’re trying to get your degree,
The nice professor didn’t charge a fee.
You hopped on a ship heading out to sea,
Your impending doom you did not foresee.

You have no special skills or pow'rs at all,
But then you’re tough enough to bear a fall.
Now that you’re backed up 'gainst a cold stone wall,
The big Bad Sphinx wants you to join the brawl.

To 'liminate the mafia is your goal;
You have a voice, a vote, too, and your soul.
But first finish the food still in your bowl,
Now good luck on finding the Bad Sphinx’s moles.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote. You win when all the Mafia are dead, so your goal should be to find them.

The game thread can be found here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Field Medic (Town Doctor) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Field Medic (Town Doctor).

You’re just a doctor with a first-aid kit,
But you’re far better than you will admit.
You can fix everything from tiny slits
To huge turbulent epileptic fits.

You joined the trip because you thought it'd bring
Much money, fame, and all those sorts of things.
But little did you know that you’d be jinx’d,
Inadvertently waking the Bad Sphinx.

Now you will have to think and use your mind
To get yourself out of this sin'ster bind.
The mafia are the ones whom you must find
If you don’t wanna get shot from behind.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then protect them for the night and prevent them from dying. You can also save yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row. You win when all the Mafia are dead, so your goal should be to find them.

The game thread can be found here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Anubis Statue (Town Tracker) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Anubis Statue (Town Tracker).

You were created a long time ago,
To prevent the rise of your ancient foe.
But now the Sphinx again says oh, hello,
With schemes to fill the world with endless woe.

The disruption has shaken you awake,
And now you have strong cravings for a cake.
But this is not the time for you to bake:
You must correct the explorers’ mistake.

With every night’s scream and chilling yell,
Survivors shudder as victims are fell'd.
So utilize your doggie’s sense of smell
To find the ones who cause the grisly knell.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then trace that person’s scent, tracking them, and will be informed of who that player visited or targeted, if any. You win when all the Mafia are dead, so your goal should be to find them.

The game thread can be found here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Ancient Egyptian Priest (Town Jailkeeper) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Ancient Egyptian Priest (Town Jailkeeper).

You hail from a long since forgotten past,
When the Bad Sphinx was th'roughly beat'n at last.
But there's a prophecy that you had heard:
The Sphinx will rise, will once again be stirred.

Still worrying about this day and night,
You wanted once again to join the fight.
You resolved to perform an ancient rite,
To skip through time all in a flash of light.

Unfortunately you arrived too late,
For the explorers have taken the bait.
Now you will join them in their big debate,
And see if you can twice defy the Fates.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then cast a spell on them and turn them to stone for the night, blocking any night actions that your target attempts perform, but also protecting them from attack. You may not target yourself with this ability. You win when all the Mafia are dead, so your goal should be to find them.

The game thread can be found here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Old Archaeology Professor (Town Cop) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Old Archaeology Professor (Town Cop).

The studying of artifacts for years,
Has taught you much but leaves still much unclear.
This was to be the peak of your career,
And you'd recruited several volunteers.

No need to worry; there's nothing to fear,
Yes, that’s what you had said over a beer.
And you’d describe the Sphinx, but you would jeer,
It doesn't exist, you'd said so with a sneer.

Unfortunately you turned out quite wrong,
The Sphinx is real and is extremely strong.
There're two among you who do not belong,
So find them, else this will be your swan song.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then look through your target’s bag for any clues to their intentions and will be informed of their alignment. You may also receive a "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving. You win when all the Mafia are dead, so your goal should be to find them.

The game thread can be found here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Mercenary (1-shot Bulletproof Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Mercenary (1-shot Bulletproof Townie).

Once a soldier in the Second World War,
You’ve seen the blood, the corpses, and much more.
The bulletproof vest to battles you wore
You ne'er remove once you leave your front door.

The promised vet'ran’s pay had never come,
So you must dwell in a small run-down slum.
No, not content thus, you wanted income,
Hence mercenary you had then become.

Your caution has been worth, you have not erred,
Despite those who mocked you and called you scared.
Two imposters 'mong you, the Sphinx declared,
But you can laugh, for you are well prepared.

You will survive a single attack, but after that, if you are attacked again, you will be killed. You are also not immune to the daily hang, your ability cannot be roleblocked, and you do not know when you lose your bulletproof vest. You win when all the Mafia are dead, so your goal should be to find them.

The game thread can be found here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Grave Robber (Mafia Goon) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Grave Robber (Mafia Goon).

Since childhood, you’ve feared an empty purse.
Graverobbing filled it, but gave you a curse.
You’ve gone to a psychic, and to a nurse.
Won’t work, the Sphinx told you, it'll make it worse.

Here is a deal, I’ll break the curse, he said.
Else you will have to wait until you’re dead.
But what’s the catch, the price? you asked in dread.
A human sacrifice so I'll be fed.

Who'll help us find the tomb? the trav'lers asked:
A perfect chance, and then the bait was cast.
Now here we are, before the Sphinx at last,
Without bein' found, it's time to have a blast.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this QuickTopic while you are alive. Together, you may discuss and submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal should be to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you.

The game thread can be found here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Hello, [player]!

You are the Toilet Paper Mummy (Mafia Roleblocker).

You were a mighty pharaoh long ago,
Whom rebels plotted hard to overthrow.
And in a war we now no longer know,
You were sent packing to the world below.

Although the linen has long since decayed,
The Sphinx has revived you to join the fray.
Some toilet paper'll do, he said one day,
I will go purchase it without delay.

Now the explorers have nearly arrived,
And all of them are very much alive.
You with your partner you must connive,
To change this fact, and also to survive.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then wrap your target in a cocoon of toilet paper for the night, roleblocking that person for that night and preventing their night actions, if any, from resolving.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal should be to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you.

[center][size=200]Welcome to Mafia Game 217[/size]

[center][hide=Story][i]A gaggle of explorers, 
Hats and torches right at hand, 
Journeyed to the land of Egypt, 
Also called the land of sand,  
Where the men drool over treasures
Cuz the food is way too bland.

[hide=- Game Rules -]
[b]This tab is a summary of the most important rules in this game.[/b]

[b]If you are an experienced player:[/b] you can skip everything other than this tab and the setup tab. 
[b]If you are new to the game:[/b] it is recommended that you at least skim through everything. The most important rules are in the three tabs under Game Rules and the Conduct Guidelines tab. Welcome to Mafia! 

[list=1][*] [b]Do not edit or delete[/b] your posts, even for typos. Just post again. 
[*] Each "day" phase in this game is [b]2 weeks in real life[/b] while "night" lasts [b]3 days[/b].
[*] [b]Do not post here during night.[/b]
[*] You [b]must format your votes correctly[/b]. If a vote wasn't counted, it was probably formatted incorrectly. Neither divergent capitalizations of usernames nor capital i/lowercase L are differentiated; manipulations of either still count as valid votes. 
[*] [b]Majority hang is off[/b] for Day 1, but it is [b]on[/b] starting Day 2.
[*] [b]Deadline hang is on[/b] from Day 1 but it is [b]off[/b] for LYLO. 
[*] Votes are [b]locked at LYLO[/b].
[*] [b]Do not OOT (Out of Thread)[/b] and talk about this game outside your role PM, QT's, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator QT will be granted upon request. 
[*] This game uses [b]Dawn start;[/b] Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules, speaking of which, if you've read all the rules below and are ready to start, say something with at least three words that rhyme. 
[*] All [b]times[/b] will be given in [b]Eastern Standard Time[/b] (EST, UTC -5) or [b]Eastern Daylight Time[/b] (EDT, UTC -4), as applicable. 
[*] Activity rules for this game are [b]48 hours to in-game prod[/b] and [b]72 hours to PM prod.[/b]
[*] [b]Use common sense.[/b] 
[*] [b]All other rules are standard.[/b] If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned. 
[*] This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. Please PM the mods to request access. 
[*] Best of luck, and have fun! 
[/hide]

[list][*][hide=General]
[i]Credits to [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/user/93494]Binomial-theorem[/url], [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/user/89198]chaotic_iak[/url], [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/user/90250]willwang123[/url], and [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/user/144193]fdas[/url], among others, as well as the [url=https://www.mafiauniverse.com/forums/threads/27-Forum-Rules]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url], for the rule template. [/i]

[b]The following are the standard mafia game rules.[/b]


[list][*] Do not post during the night phase.
[*] Do not quote or pretend to quote any PM's from the mods, including role PM's.
[*] Do not talk about this game outside of this thread to anyone other than the game moderators, unless you are specifically given permission to do so. 
[*] Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
[*] Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [verbatim][color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent][/verbatim], among others. 
[*] Hide tags are allowed as long as they are visible. 
[*] Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary. 
[*] If you are dead, do not post in this thread, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and you will be granted access to the spectator QT. 
[*] Do not impersonate the mods, such as by spoofing votecounts or EOD posts. 
[*] Do not lie to the mods. 
[*] Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be taken into account during rule enforcement. 
[*] You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications. Additional details can be found in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[b]This tab covers voting, unvoting, hangs, and No Hangs. [/b]


[list][*] Vote in bold font in this format: [b]Vote: [player username][/b]. A vote will not be counted if it does not follow this exact format. 
[*] The capitalization of the player name does not matter, but the exact spelling does. Do not use nicknames. 
[*] The one exception to the above rule is the use of I (capital i) or l (lowercase L). They are considered identical for the purposes of voting. 
[*] For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes. However, [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjayy.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are not valid votes. 
[*] Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*] You can remove a previously made vote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before casting a new vote. 
[*] Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. If you want to purposely fakevote, please try to notify the mods in your role PM so that it will not be accidentally counted.
[*] This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EoD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged. 
[*] If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*] Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately. 
[*] Upon reaching LYLO, (Hang-or-Lose), deadline hang will be turned off. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*] You may also choose on any given day to not hang anyone and No Hang instead by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b]. 
[*] If an even number of players are alive, half of the players voting for No Hang will be considered a majority. This only applies to No Hang. 
[*] The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted, even if the moderators have not yet made a post declaring the end of day. If this happens, the game will proceed into twilight. 
[*] During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be counted.
[*] Once the moderators declare the end of day, the person hanged will be officially dead, and their role will be revealed. Night will then begin immediately. 
[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements. [/b]


[list][*] All times and deadlines will be given in the time currently observed on the U.S. East Coast, namely Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*] Each day phase will last 336 hours (14 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of day at their discretion, usually in cases of acute inactivity or replacements too close to the deadline. 
[*] Each night phase will last 72 hours (3 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of night at their discretion. 
[*] Exact deadlines will be specified at the start of each phase. 
[*] In determining whether votes cast near the deadline are valid, the moderators will reference the time-of-posting given by the AoPS system. Votes logged as being cast at or after the specified EOD time will not be valid. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*] During the night phase, players with unsubmitted, available NA's (night actions) will be sent a PM 36 to 24 hours before the deadline, reminding them to submit their actions. 
[*] If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player - or their partner(s) if applicable - by the night phase deadline, the player will not perform an NA that night. NA submissions made after the night deadline has passed will not be valid. 
[*] Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*] Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline. 
[*] Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence). Please post V/LAs in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in your role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*] V/LA's should be less than a week long. For longer absences, please contact the moderators for more options. 
[*] Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request a replacement by posting the following in-thread or in their role PM: [b]Request Replacement[/b]. 
[*] Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*] Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*] Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*] Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours (4.5 days) and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*] For details on what constitutes a "game-advancing post," please refer to the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*] If a player receives 5 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*] If a player receives a postban from the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. For more details, please go to the Moderation Policies tab. 
[list][/hide][/list]
[hide=- Conduct Guidelines -]
[b]This tab is a summary of the tabs following this one, concerning concepts vital to healthy gameplay.[/b]

[b]Important note:[/b] If you ever find that this game is stressing you out or causing you frustration, please know that, as the moderators, we are here for you. We know that not being able to talk to other people about this game due to OOT rules can feel suffocating at times, so please feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can talk and listen, or even help you find a better way to cope, such as by taking a break from the game. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM as well. 

[list=1][*] Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition. 
[*] Discussions must stay in this thread. Attaching files or linking to external documents concerning this game is not allowed. 
[*] Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games. 
[*] Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read. 
[*] You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves. 
[*] First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2. [/list]
[/hide]

[list][*][hide=Sportsmanship]
[b]This tab covers concepts that are conducive to healthy gameplay and the steps moderators may take to intervene if things go wrong.[/b] 

Most players will not encounter the situations detailed below and in most cases will not be warned even if they do not observe these guidelines. 

[i]This section will be updated periodically. If this happens while a game is in progress, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP. [/i]


[list][*] Do not gamethrow.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] hard claiming as a mafia for no reason, outing your teammates, posting screenshots of the Mafia QT, hanging a conftown as town
[b]This does not include:[/b] giving up, strategic suiciding, lolcatting, or self-voting

You should play towards your wincondition(s) to the best of your ability. Intentionally sabotaging the game for any reason - such as to troll, out of a personal vendetta, or out of frustration - is prohibited. Moderator action will differ based on context, but will likely be severe, ranging from a force modkill to a forum ban. Please note that only intentionally playing against your wincon and hurting your teammates is banned - you are otherwise allowed to play however you like, and examples such as giving up, being unhelpful, or making mistakes in good faith will not prompt intervention. 

[b]Important note:[/b] According to decisions made by forum moderators [url=https://aops.com/community/p15771679]detailed in this post[/url], anything that appears to be gamethrowing - whether real or fake - is not allowed. Any serious statement along the lines of "I am actually mafia and/or my partner is [player]" that appears to be made with the intent to gamethrow will result in an immediate modkill, even if the statement is false. As this subject requires nuanced knowledge of the difference between lolcatting, gamethrowing, and the like, we recommend avoiding this type of situation entirely until you have enough experience to navigate the fine line between the two. 


[list][*] Publicly discussing player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other moderator interactions is discouraged.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] "[player] was probably force replaced for trying to PM their mafia partner."
[b]This does not include:[/b] discussing lurking or player activity in reference to their alignment

Seriously discussing a player's alignment based off of their V/LAs, replacements, warnings, or other essential moderator communications is highly discouraged. Speculation, jokes, or hot takes about such events are okay, but if a serious discussion occurs in the thread, the moderators may step in and ask participants to stop. Repeatedly refusing to listen to the moderators may result in a warning. 


[list][*] Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels are prohibited. [/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] an encrypted file only certain players possess the key to, the use of cryptosystems, using encryption programs to hide messages in posts
[b]This does not include:[/b] leaving breadcrumbs, manually encoded/decoded ciphers, messages in the form of a public riddle, brief conversation in non-English languages

Unless private day-communication is specifically enabled in the game, you are prohibited from attempting to communicate with another player/other players privately. This is an extension of the rule banning posts with concealed text: all game-related information should be visible and reasonably available to be accessed and/or analyzed by all players. If you do wish to establish a secret method of daytime communication with your teammates, it may be a good idea to run your idea by the moderator team, but even if you do not, you will likely only get a warning or receive a request to cease using the method in question. 


[list][*] Avoiding referencing OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or creating OGI situations that could be construed as alignment indicative is recommended. [/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] adding all game players to a quote-on-quote "unrelated" PM, "[player] is really active in their other games, but not this one. Are they lurking as maf?" or "[player] and [player] have been acting unusually friendly in [this unrelated forum], I bet they're actually the scumteam."
[b]This does not include:[/b] mentioning real life, online interactions, and other unrelated comments as long as they are alignment agnostic

OGI (Out of Game Information) is exactly what it stands for - anything that happens outside of the game thread - and it is only problematic when it is used alignment-indicatively. The most important things to remember are these: a) do not publicly discuss a read based on OGI, and b) try to avoid situations outside the game thread that could cause other players to suspect your alignment, town or mafia. Everything else is fine, and mentioning things that happened outside the thread or in real life in the game thread is also fine, but it must be alignment agnostic, or reasonably unable to be used to determine your alignment. The purpose of this is to preserve game integrity by minimizing outside influences. The moderators may intervene to stop an OGI discussion or incident and ask participants to cease. Repeated failure to listen to the moderators may result in a warning. 


[list][*] Avoiding formatting that makes text hard to read is recommended.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] bright colors, [verbatim][size=1], [hide=.], [tip][hide][/verbatim]
[b]This does not include:[/b] colored VCAs, colored responses in quotes, small hide tags used to hide a joke

As long as a given text is not invisible or effectively so, no warnings will be issued. However, if players complain or if this is abused, the moderators may step in and ask the participant(s) to stop. 
[/hide]

[*][hide=Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers player etiquette, warning limits, and things players should avoid doing. [/b]


[list][*] Do not make personal attacks.
[*] Do not post excessive spam. 
[*] Other behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning. 
[*] Abide by AoPS’s Community Guidelines. 
[*] Be mature and use common sense in disputes. 
[*] For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction, depending on the severity. If a player breaks a rule after they have received their maximum number of warnings, or if they commit a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced. [/list]

In addition to these rules, please note the types of posts that are implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are now liable to be warned for these posts. This should have little to no effect on normal player behavior: this enforcement policy has been created to better define the boundaries of the "do not abuse loopholes" rule and to encourage healthier gameplay. 

[hide=Discouraged Player Conduct]
[b]This tab provides detailed examples of posts that may result in a warning. [/b]

[i]This section will be updated periodically. If this happens while a game is in progress, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP. [/i]


[list]
[*] Posts with formatting that imitates the text indicating that a post has been edited. 
[*] Posts that are blank or otherwise contain no content. 
[*] Unsolicited advertising. 
[*] Multiple posts with identical content. 
[*] Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text. 
[*] Extended and in-depth communication in code or non-English languages. 
[/list]

Posts in this category typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met: 


[list=1]
[*] The player possesses a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior
[*] The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content
[*] The posts have caused notably negative reactions from other players
[/list]

Again, the majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines or receive a warning even if they break them. This is simply to better and more transparently enforce the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that are not explicitly forbidden by previous rulesets.
[/hide]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderation Policies]
[b]This tab provides more detail on how the moderation team will handle notable fringe scenarios. [/b]


[list][*]Role Randomization[/list]

When randomizing roles/alignments at the start of a game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. As a result, both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are possible. 

[hide=Current RNG Process (Matrix6)]
All randomizations are done by [url]random.org[/url]. 

Once the playerlist has been finalized, the setup will be randomized using the [url=https://www.random.org/integers/]Random Integer Generator[/url]: 

[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/708770843731165224/762748231598997534/unknown.png[/img]

The numbers correspond to the order in which the 6 possible setups are listed in the Setup tab. 

Once a setup has been chosen, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, Town PRs, Mafia Goon(s), Mafia PR. This list will be randomized using the [url=https://www.random.org/lists/]List Randomizer[/url] and overlaid on top of the playerlist, which is ordered first by descending rank and then by alphabetical order. The ordered playerlist of each game can be found in the Alive/Dead tabs. [/hide]


[list][*]Tiebreaking[/list]

If a two-way tie is reached at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made on Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made on Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This solution was chosen to avoid tiebreaking via RNG while simultaneously preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting. Oh, and by the way, after actually looking through the rules this time and not cheating, PM the moderators the name of a random color and do not leak this rule again. 

The same solution will be applied if a three-way tie or higher is reached at deadline. 


[list][*]Votes cast during deadline[/list]

If votes or unvotes are cast before or during the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the official logged post times. These can be found immediately by locating the relevant post at https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/u[account ID of player] and referencing the upper-right corner of the applicable entry. 

Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes shown as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not be counted. 


[list][*]Game-advancing Posts[/list]

A "game-advancing post" is literally anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. For consistency purposes, most posts other than those reading "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks. 


[list][*]Postbans[/list]

After recent clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, the moderators are no longer allowed to proxy posts from players who have been postbanned. As a result, once a player is postbanned, they will have to be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation, such as if the postban is less than a week in length. 

Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help. 

You may contact the moderators through discord at [b]ashton#1850[/b] or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS. 


[list][*]Outside Consulting[/list]

If the moderators have trouble resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity, they may contact a forum mod or other respected moderator for help resolving the incident. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the course of actions the moderators chose, you may request that the moderators consult for a second opinion, if they have not done so already. 
[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*] mathjay and Socoobo are the moderators of Mafia Game 217. 
[*] HannahW and Mathletesv contributed to flavor, OCRSTM, feedback, and the management of the private chat discord system. 
[*] This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be given private keys granting access to the server as appropriate. 
[*] [color=#FF0000]Red-colored text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other official announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
[*] [color=#0000FF]Blue-colored text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*] [color=#00FF00]Green-colored text[/color] is used for town flavor-related posts.
[*] Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*] Current custom shades are: Town flavor green ([color=#00f752]#00f752[/color]), Activity Check blue ([color=#005dff]#005dff[/color]), mathjay personal blue ([color=#4a86e8]#4a86e8[/color]), HannahW personal blue ([color=#0b5394]#0b5394[/color]), Mathletesv personal blue ([color=#4d94ff]#4d94ff[/color]), Socoobo personal orange ([color=#ff8000]#ff8000[/color]).
[*] The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*] If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days or by the next applicable EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*] The moderators may change the rules at any time, but major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OP changelog. The thing you're looking for is this: put a smiley somewhere. Most rule changes will be designed to have as little effect on normal, day-to-day play as possible and will be designed to streamline gameplay. 
[*] Players will not be retroactively penalized for violations of new rules made before the changes have been announced. 
[*] Major changes to the OP will be made in between games. The Version History tab will list the most important changes. 
[*] The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make final judgement calls on the moderation of this game. [/list]

[b]MG#217 OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset$^\text{TM}$ (OCRSTM) version 4.[/b]
[i]Current version: 4.1.1[/i]
[i]OCRSTM is written and maintained by the Unpaid Intern$^\text{TM}$ Moderator Team. [/i][/hide][/list]
[hide=Setup]
This game is based on the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Matrix6]Matrix6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
[list][*] [b]Setup 1:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] [b]Setup 2:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] [b]Setup 3:[/b] 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] [b]Setup A:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] [b]Setup B:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] [b]Setup C:[/b] 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/list]
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list][*] This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then. 
[*] Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
[*] If applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
[*] A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock in the same night.
[*] Cop results are sane. 
[*] Votes are locked at LYLO. 
[*] Mafia do not have daychat and may only discuss in the designated Quicktopic during night.[/list]

[hide=Role PMs]
[quote="Archaeology Grad Student (Vanilla Townie)"]
Hello, [player]!

You are an [color=#00f752]Archaeology Grad Student (Vanilla Townie)[/color].

Because you’re trying to get your degree,
The nice professor didn’t charge a fee.
You hopped on a ship heading out to sea,
Your impending doom you did not foresee.

You have no special skills or pow'rs at all,
But then you’re tough enough to bear a fall.
Now that you’re backed up 'gainst a cold stone wall,
The big Bad Sphinx wants you to join the brawl.

To 'liminate the mafia is your goal;
You have a voice, a vote, too, and your soul.
But first finish the food still in your bowl,
Now good luck on finding the Bad Sphinx’s moles.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote. You win when all the Mafia are dead, so your goal should be to find them. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
[/quote] 

[quote="Field Medic (Town Doctor)"]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Field Medic (Town Doctor)[/color].

You’re just a doctor with a first-aid kit,
But you’re far better than you will admit.
You can fix everything from tiny slits
To huge turbulent epileptic fits.

You joined the trip because you thought it'd bring
Much money, fame, and all those sorts of things.
But little did you know that you’d be jinx’d,
Inadvertently waking the Bad Sphinx.

Now you will have to think and use your mind
To get yourself out of this sin'ster bind.
The mafia are the ones whom you must find
If you don’t wanna get shot from behind.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then protect them for the night and prevent them from dying. You can also save yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row. You win when all the Mafia are dead, so your goal should be to find them. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[quote="Anubis Statue (Town Tracker)"]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Anubis Statue (Town Tracker)[/color].

You were created a long time ago,
To prevent the rise of your ancient foe.
But now the Sphinx again says oh, hello,
With schemes to fill the world with endless woe.

The disruption has shaken you awake,
And now you have strong cravings for a cake.

You must correct the explorers’ mistake.

With every night’s scream and chilling yell,

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then trace that person’s scent, tracking them, and will be informed of who that player visited or targeted, if any. You win when all the Mafia are dead, so your goal should be to find them.

Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Ancient Egyptian Priest (Town Jailkeeper)[/color].

You hail from a long since forgotten past,
When the Bad Sphinx was th'roughly beat'n at last.
But there's a prophecy that you had heard:
The Sphinx will rise, will once again be stirred.

Still worrying about this day and night,
You wanted once again to join the fight.
You resolved to perform an ancient rite,
To skip through time all in a flash of light.

Unfortunately you arrived too late,
For the explorers have taken the bait.
Now you will join them in their big debate,

The studying of artifacts for years,

This was to be the peak of your career,

No need to worry; there's nothing to fear,

Automatic ISO Code
Written by Mathletesv and reposted with permission.

ISO Code
Number.prototype.between = function(a, b, inclusive) {
  var min = Math.min(a, b),
    max = Math.max(a, b);
min -=1, max += 1;
 
  return inclusive ? this >= min && this <= max : this > min && this < max;
}
function number_matches(quote_box,start,end) {
return (parseInt($(quote_box).parents(".cmty-post-tr").children(".cmty-post-middle").children(".cmty-post-top-inline.cmty-post-top-data").children(".cmty-post-top").children(".cmty-post-number").text().slice(1)).between(start,end) )
    }
function user_matches (quote_box,username) {
    return ($(quote_box).parent().parent().children(".cmty-post-left").children(".cmty-post-username").text() == username)
    }

user = prompt("What is the username of the person you are iso'ing?");
min = parseInt(prompt("First number of range"), 10);
max = parseInt(prompt("Second number of range"), 10);
$(".cmty-post-quote").each(function () {
    if (user_matches(this, user) && number_matches(this, min, max)) {
        $(this).click()
    }
    })


Source


Description: given a username and a range of posts to look in, this code will automatically quote all posts from the specified player into the reply field.

Note: this code does not work on Android, iOS, or any other device without a javascript web console.

Instructions
Step 1: Load the posts you want to ISO.
The code cannot retrieve a player’s posts if you do not have them currently loaded in your browser, so you must scroll down (or up) through the thread to load the posts you want in your ISO. Scrolling can be done using your keyboard’s Page Down or Page Up buttons. AoPS also periodically refuses to load posts when scrolling up; if this happens, “bounce” the thread by scrolling down for a bit before scrolling back up.
Step 2: Open your browser’s console.
For Chrome, the shortcut is Control + Shift + J (Windows, Linux, Chrome OS) or Command + Option + J (macOS). For Safari, first enable Developer Menu by going to Safari Menu > Preferences > Advanced tab and checking “Show Develop menu in menu bar,” then use the shortcut Command + Option + C. For Firefox, the shortcut is Control + Shift + K (Windows, Linux) or Command + Option + K (macOS). For Microsoft Edge, the shortcut is Command + Option + J (Windows).
Step 3: Copy/paste the code into the console and hit Enter.
Once you do so, the code will prompt you in a popup and you will not be able to access the tab you are running the code on. Do not switch to another tab in the same window, or the code will not work.
Step 4: Enter the username of the player you are ISOing.
This is case-sensitive; capitalize and spell the username correctly.
Step 5: Enter the first post of your ISO range.
If you are doing a full ISO, you can use 1 or 0. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number from which you want the code to search for a player’s posts.
Step 6: Enter the last post of your ISO range.
If you are doing a full ISO, enter any number larger than the thread postcount; 10000 works. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number to which you want the code to search for a player’s posts. Please note that posts that are within the range you have specified, but that have not been loaded in your browser, will not be included by the code in the ISO.

Once you have done so, the reply field will open, with all the relevant posts quoted in it.


Important notes:
  • The username is case sensitive.
  • The min/max limits of the range do not have to be exact.
  • The first range input must be smaller than the second range input.
  • This code does not work in [pywindow].
  • You cannot access the contents of the tab you are running the code in while it is running, so remember to memorize the target username before running the code.
  • You can rerun the code once it has finished running.
  • You can run the code even if you have a reply partly written.
  • If the code refuses to work, check that all of the above has been done correctly. If it still does not work, reload the tab and try again.

[hide=Automatic ISO Code]
[b]Written by [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/user/417841]Mathletesv[/url] and reposted with permission.[/b]

[hide=ISO Code][code]
Number.prototype.between = function(a, b, inclusive) {
  var min = Math.min(a, b),
    max = Math.max(a, b);
min -=1, max += 1;
 
  return inclusive ? this >= min && this <= max : this > min && this < max;
}
function number_matches(quote_box,start,end) {
return (parseInt($(quote_box).parents(".cmty-post-tr").children(".cmty-post-middle").children(".cmty-post-top-inline.cmty-post-top-data").children(".cmty-post-top").children(".cmty-post-number").text().slice(1)).between(start,end) )
    }
function user_matches (quote_box,username) {
    return ($(quote_box).parent().parent().children(".cmty-post-left").children(".cmty-post-username").text() == username)
    }

user = prompt("What is the username of the person you are iso'ing?");
min = parseInt(prompt("First number of range"), 10);
max = parseInt(prompt("Second number of range"), 10);
$(".cmty-post-quote").each(function () {
    if (user_matches(this, user) && number_matches(this, min, max)) {
        $(this).click()
    }
    })
[/code]

[url=https://aops.com/community/p16738769]Source[/url][/hide]

[b]Description:[/b] given a username and a range of posts to look in, this code will automatically quote all posts from the specified player into the reply field. 

[i]Note: this code does not work on Android, iOS, or any other device without a javascript web console.[/i]

[hide=Instructions]
[b]Step 1: Load the posts you want to ISO.[/b]
The code cannot retrieve a player’s posts if you do not have them currently loaded in your browser, so you must scroll down (or up) through the thread to load the posts you want in your ISO. Scrolling can be done using your keyboard’s Page Down or Page Up buttons. AoPS also periodically refuses to load posts when scrolling up; if this happens, “bounce” the thread by scrolling down for a bit before scrolling back up. 
[b]Step 2: Open your browser’s console. [/b]
For Chrome, the shortcut is [b]Control + Shift + J[/b] (Windows, Linux, Chrome OS) or [b]Command + Option + J[/b] (macOS). For Safari, first enable Developer Menu by going to Safari Menu > Preferences > Advanced tab and checking “Show Develop menu in menu bar,” then use the shortcut [b]Command + Option + C[/b]. For Firefox, the shortcut is [b]Control + Shift + K[/b] (Windows, Linux) or [b]Command + Option + K[/b] (macOS). For Microsoft Edge, the shortcut is [b]Command + Option + J[/b] (Windows). 
[b]Step 3: Copy/paste the code into the console and hit Enter.[/b]
Once you do so, the code will prompt you in a popup and you will not be able to access the tab you are running the code on. Do not switch to another tab in the same window, or the code will not work. 
[b]Step 4: Enter the username of the player you are ISOing. [/b]
This is case-sensitive; capitalize and spell the username correctly. 
[b]Step 5: Enter the first post of your ISO range.[/b]
If you are doing a full ISO, you can use 1 or 0. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number from which you want the code to search for a player’s posts. 
[b]Step 6: Enter the last post of your ISO range.[/b]
If you are doing a full ISO, enter any number larger than the thread postcount; 10000 works. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number to which you want the code to search for a player’s posts. Please note that posts that are within the range you have specified, but that have not been loaded in your browser, will not be included by the code in the ISO. 

Once you have done so, the reply field will open, with all the relevant posts quoted in it. [/hide]

Important notes: 
[list][*] The username is case sensitive. 
[*] The min/max limits of the range do not have to be exact. 
[*] The first range input must be smaller than the second range input. 
[*] This code does not work in [verbatim][pywindow][/verbatim].
[*] You cannot access the contents of the tab you are running the code in while it is running, so remember to memorize the target username before running the code. 
[*] You can rerun the code once it has finished running. 
[*] You can run the code even if you have a reply partly written. 
[*] If the code refuses to work, check that all of the above has been done correctly. If it still does not work, reload the tab and try again. [/list]
[/hide]

post_rendered:
Forensics evidence / Group 0 / Codename: Dawn

Exhibit 0.A - Digital document / Origin: investigators' encrypted file dropbox / Date: 03/12/2021
Once upon a modern time,
Quite near the present day,
An expedition organized
With haste and marched away.

Their men were bold their women fair
None were more rash than they
They couldn’t even possibly
be slightly more cliche.

Tis no surprise they found themselves,
to their clam'rous dismay,
Widely mocked and ridiculed
from Egypt to Bombay.

Despite this fact they had big dreams
To fill their resume,
With tales of journeying to find,
an actual tomb someday

So blah blah blah blah they set out
With uniforms passe,
Packing cheezits and some crackers
For a midnight soiree.

Their spirits and their hopes so high,
They touched the milky way,
Not once did it occur to them,
They might’ve been led astray.

A group of ragtag explorers,
Two fiends making their play,
A couple friendly spirits,
And then evil’s attache,
A Boss most multi-talented,
One unpaid protege.
What could be the outcome?
Only Fate could truly say.


Exhibit 0.B - Papyrus document, translated / Origin: preserved scroll, part of collection from Library of Alexandria / Date: unknown
Long, long ago, in forgotten old times,
There once was Sphinx who was good at rhymes,
But try as he might, he could not write a single riddle,
So in his frustration he went quite medieval,
And found a solution barbaric and evil
And he stumped passerby with many a tarradiddle.

The greatest of mankind attempted to beat him,
But none ever managed to truly defeat him,
Cuz the answers he wanted were so always laughably false,
And many a scholar wrote many a scroll,
On how to evade the substantial toll,
But no one yet knew how to answer the verses in valse.

Such terror he caused, so widely was he famed,
As the Bad Sphinx he was officially named,
He’s gone, by the way, there’s no reason to currently flee.
But we don’t know how he was so thoroughly trounced,
For the heroes who’ve done so have bafflingly bounced,
So remember to pray that he’s forever in absentee.


Exhibit 0.C - Digital document / Origin: investigators' encrypted file dropbox / Date: 03/18/2021

After days of scanning the cliffs,
They have finally found some glyphs.
Following these symbols, they got a whiff,
Of old corpses and got scared stiff.

With only a flashlight to light the way,
From the scorching sun they got away.
But through the tunnels they were led astray,
By the impostors who wanted to betray.

To a large chamber they were led,
Only to see a giant cat’s head.
The light went out and they saw red;
Missing was their leader’s head.

Socoobo has died. He was the Expedition Leader (Moderator 2).

The blood had spilled onto the floor,
And instantly awoke the Sphinx once more.
Let’s play a game, he said with a roar,
I wouldn't want to be a bore!

There are two amongst you that helped me wake,
Find and kill them or the world will break.
I’ll destroy civilization, I’ll make the earth quake,
And I’ll give you all a huge headache.

But before doomsday, come, riddle me this,
Cuz without a puzzle I would be remiss.
Although I am rusty; it's quite hit or miss.
So what is a lifeboat and also coulisse?

The poor bankrupt intern in the tomb,
Having the other thesaurus in the room,
Yelped, Wood is the answer, or I'm a legume,
I answered your riddle, now please just go boom?

The Sphinx then smiled, a quite scary sight,
Saying, I will admit that you're probably right,
But I'm the Bad Sphynx and right answers I spite.
The nonsensical answer is therefore Egg White.

Oh jeez, said the intern, my words I append,
That never happened, please, can we pretend?
Nope, said the Sphynx, proceeding to descend,
And making quick work of the intern's poor end.

mathjay has died. He was the Unpaid Intern (Moderator 1).

Now off you go, he said, you have lots to do,
You can’t solve the puzzle without a clue.
Since I’m so generous, here’s some stew,
I’ll see you soon, but for now I’ll bid you adieu.


Dawn has started on October 11, 2020 - 9:00 AM.

Please read the Game Rules and Conduct Guidelines tabs for the confirm rule. It will not say "confirm rule," but will instead tell you to do something. Day 1 will start once everyone confirms.

post_rendered:
Hey sudz

post_rendered:
myself wrote:
Each game begins in a low/no information state. Some players will have already posted, and some have yet to show up. To get the game moving, most games will start with some RVS and RQS. RVS stands for Random Voting Stage. This is where players will choose to vote others for with little or no reasoning. The purpose of this is to obtain information through these votes. For example, someone might react a little over defensive to a couple of votes, some players might seem a little too overeager to wagon people, or you might notice someone still acting a bit silly after everyone else has stopped joking. All of these actions give information, and help to move the game into a more serious state. Similarly, RQS stands for Random Question Stage. This is where someone may ask a series of questions to the rest of the players. The purpose of this is twofold. First, it helps to break the ice in a way and help players to get to know each other better. Second, it might also provoke interesting, informational reactions. Generally, RVS is better for providing information and RQS is more used as a get-to-know-you activity.
