what do you think the V in RVS stands for lol

the entire purpose of RVS is that you throw votes around and call more things alignment indicative than you really need to. in general you want your votes to utilize prior info that you think is alignment indicative--only problem is, that info has to come from somewhere.

i'd give an example here but i don't think i'm supposed to while we're waiting for everyone to show up so i will hold off on it for now


but yeah like midway was saying, it's pretty much always a good idea to vote someone out in a day. the reasoning is pretty simple: when mafia does a kill, it's guaranteed to hit town. when a hang happens is the only time a mafia can feasibly die. by not hanging on day 1, town mechanically drops the number of hangs they get a chance to do by 1. (yes this math ignores doc saves and stuff that could possibly prolong it. don't expect those to happen though; it's a nice thing when they happen, that's all.)
it's a pretty significant loss to lose a hang opportunity, so we need to hang someone on d1, even if it might possibly be suboptimal due to not enough info on everyone.

but yeah like midway was saying, it's pretty much always a good idea to vote someone out in a day. the reasoning is pretty simple: when mafia does a kill, it's guaranteed to hit town. when a hang happens is the only time a mafia can feasibly die. by not hanging on day 1, town mechanically drops the number of hangs they get a chance to do by 1. (yes this math ignores doc saves and stuff that could possibly prolong it. don't expect those to happen though; it's a nice thing when they happen, that's all.)
it's a pretty significant loss to lose a hang opportunity, so we need to hang someone on d1, even if it might possibly be suboptimal due to not enough info on everyone.

this also ties into a strategy called process of elimination, or poe for short
you generally have two factions, a towncore of people who are thought of as consensus town, and poe which is full of people not thought of as consensus town
a sure way to win games is to eliminate poe until there's no one left, which usually results in all scum being hung unless they play extremely well
hangs will always be in poe, while scum kills will always be in towncore
we want to maximize the number of people killed in poe, and the only way to do that is to hang people

It’ll be fun, they said,
You’ll learn a tonne, they said,
You’ll be so happy you did it once you’re done, they said.

But they didn’t mention spiders,
Or the flies you can’t eschew,
Or the occasional scorpion
That’s hiding in your shoe.
Then there’s hawkers, thieves and cannibals,
They want some of you too.
Because Egypt is a hunting ground,
And you’re the barbeque.

I’m so green, they said,
You lucky bean, they said,
A chance so good it's once-in-an-eocene, they said.

But can we talk about the ample
Safety protocols we have?
Nonexistent, unreliable,
Worse than a glacier soon to calve.
Our radios lie smithereened,
Our rations we now halve.
Worst of all we’re out of power,
For our last functioning nav.

You’ll be fine, they said,
Don’t you dare whine, they said,

[quiet strumming of a ukulele, a low voice singing quietly]

Fly me to the moon, cuz soon the world we know will end,
All the things that mattered, once, we’ll no longer depend.
In other worlds, we’d be free.
In other worlds, we’d be happy.

Let me sing this song, it’ll be the last thing that I do,
I’ll make something pretty ‘fore my maker’s rendezvous.
In other worlds, we would be,
In other worlds, nobody.

Hot take(especially for the newbies here): maths on no hang d1
For the newbies that want to know that math behind No Hang D1 and Mishang D1(aka why no hang on D1 is bad) here ya go
(made for a 7 town, 2 scum setup)
Town doesn't hang D1:
N1: Scum gets guaranteed townie nk which puts the game at 6 town and 2 scum
D2: 25% chance of hitting scum, lets assume its another mishang. This puts the totals at 5 town 2 scum
N2: Scum gets another townie nk putting the game at 4 town 2 scum
D3: Town must no hang in order to get LYLO which increases their chance at picking mafia(current percentage of hitting scum: 33%)
if town mishangs D3 on MYLO then scum just nk's a random person and they win easy
So in the case of town no hangs D1 then we only get 1 mishang
Town mishangs D1:
N1: Scum get townie nk, putting totals at 5 town 2 scum
D2: Assuming mishang, we get totals of 4 town and 2 scum at end of day(28.5% chance at hitting scum here)
N2: Scum get townie nk, putting totals at LYLO or 3 town 2 scum
D3: Lylo, with 40% chance we hit scum
So here, we get 2 hang attempts before hitting lylo with 2 scum and also the chance of hitting scum is higher with less people
thank you for coming to my ted talk

post_rendered:
Forensics evidence / Group 1 / Codename: Day 1

Exhibit 1.D - Digital document / Origin: investigators' encrypted file dropbox / Date: 03/19/2021

As night fell they lit a fire in the tomb,
With scraps they had found of a dusty broom,
And as the fire smoked did some of them fume,
“It’s probably all pog’s fault I presume.”

“In a few minutes the Sphinx will appear,
With a riddle ludicrously unclear,
Make someone answer it right then and here,
And probably end that someone’s career.”

“Who better to be that someone than pog?
So far he’s been less helpful than a log,
And he probably sneaked the last hotdog,”
Said a few people in fierce dialogue,

“It couldn’t be me,” in protest pog said,
As a cold draft colored his cheeks quite red.
“I wouldn’t stab you when you’re all in bed.
I’d never shoot you until you’re all dead!”

But the others who, wanting to survive,
With a shrug did they lazily connive,
As promptly at nine the Sphinx did arrive,
And the mood in the tomb took a nosedive.

"Hello my friends, how’s your day been?" he purred,
"Hope it wasn’t so boring you interred,
Although I did seem to hear you conferred,
pog! How about a riddle and a word?"

And although pog of course shook his head no,
The others pushed him forward with gusto,
In anticipation of the tableau,
As one of them drumrolled on a bongo,

The Sphinx raised a podium from the sand,
And a microphone popped up on the stand.
From there he asked in a manner quite grand,
A riddle for two thousand years he’d planned.

“I might be dumb and incredibly dense,
But I promise this one kind of makes sense.
Now are you ready? If so let’s commence!
Because this will be extremely intense.”

Now the Sphinx started to play a fiddle,
As he promptly jumped into his riddle.
pog stood there and gave his thumbs a twiddle,
As he awkwardly stood in the middle.

“I have only a singular vague clue,
So listen carefully, oh yes, please do.
I think this will be challenging for you,
So answer the question: what‘s one plus two?”

pog stood in place very much like a tree,
In much confusion yet still filled with glee.
It was easier than he thought it’d be,
And he answered with a simple, yelled “Three!”

“Ah,” said the Sphinx, “You would be quite correct,
If I were a normal Sphinx I suspect,
But the fact that I’m Bad you did neglect,
For the right answer is child neglect.”

And as poor pog stood hopelessly confused,
The Bad Sphinx looked quite terribly amused,
“It’s unfortunate that you were accused.
Still taking your soul though; no one’s excused.”

pog has been hanged. He was an Archaeology Grad Student (Vanilla Townie).

post_rendered:
Forensics Evidence / Group 2 / Codename: Day 2

Exhibit 2.A - Digital Document / Origin: investigators' encrypted file dropbox / Date: 03/21/2021
After the first day and the first restless night,
The explorers gathered around a campfire’s light.
Each prepared for the coming day’s fight,
While an ominous wind made notebooks take flight.

sudzoof wasn’t here yet so they had to wait.
It wasn’t like him to wake up quite so late.
The explorers got impatient and without him they ate,
Then two of them went to find out sudzoof’s fate.

They checked his tent: no one was there,
They checked his bags: they were completely bare.
They looked here and there, but he wasn’t anywhere,
It was as if he had just vanished into thin air.

“Perhaps he went into the tomb,
And perhaps that’s where he met his doom.”
“Let us not randomly assume,” another replied,
“Let us go check in the Sphinx’s room.”
For their flashlights they started to fumble,
They also heard the old statues begin to crumble.
Some of them started to grumble and mumble,
And the uneven ground caused some to stumble.

At last they came upon a lot of blood,
And saw sudzoof’s corpse covered with mud.
Tempted by the meat, the scarabs started to flood,
A shocked explorer’s flashlight hit the ground with a thud.
sudzoof has died. He was the Ancient Egyptian Priest (Town Jailkeeper).

On this note the explorer’s headed out,
Going back using the same route.
Of the danger they were in, they had no doubt,
But even so, no one had begun to pout.

The discussion begins for the second day,
Of the explorers’ potentially indefinite stay.
Will they be able to find the way,
Or will they inevitably become the Sphinx’s prey?


Exhibit 2.B - Papyrus document, translated / Origin: preserved scroll, part of collection from Library of Alexandria / Date: unknown
Oh young traveller, listen to what I have to say,
For you will need this priceless advice one day.
Here in Egypt you should never wander astray,
For then you’ll become the Bad Sphinx’s prey.

He’s a magic cat that’s two hundred feet tall,
He sometimes likes to play with a neon yellow ball.
But he can be quite vicious, he’s very willing to maul,
Because for eons no one could possibly answer his call.

Guessing riddles is his favorite game,
From this he’s gained his fame.
For all the answers are so nonsensically lame,
It’s not even fair, people often exclaim.

So yes, don’t go near him, especially not with a group,
For he’ll sneak up on you when you’re eating chicken soup.
And then he’ll tell you that imposters are sabotaging the troup,
You’ll be forced to find them or they’ll kill you in one fell swoop.

We are planning and scheming to fight him,
But the odds against us are so very grim.
We humans are unfortunately far too slim,
I’ll probably come home without a limb.

What’s that you say?
You wanna join the fray?
Why I’d never thought I’d see the day,
When someone was crazy enough to stay.

Alright then, young one,
We’ll get the job done.
Though it won’t be a lot of fun,
It’s better than having to run.

Exhibit 2.C - Paper Document / Origin: burnt notebook recovered from campsite / Date: 02/12/2021
He’s obviously scum, they said.
Don’t be a bum, they said.
He’d be suspicious to anyone who isn’t dumb, they said.

But what they didn’t mention,
Was his benevolent intention.
He just wanted his pension,
But that caused some tension.
They didn’t pay attention,
And jumped to a conclusion.

Off with his head, they said.
I wanna see red, they said.
He’s the one who’s been stealing our bread, they said.

And now that he got eaten,
Our sorrows only deepen.
Can anyone sweeten,
This horrible situation?
I’m feeling quite beaten,
I wanna go back to Sweden.

Oh heavens no, they said.
We didn’t mean to throw, they said.
He’s not a liar? That fills us with so much woe, they said.

Yeah, that’s completely right.
Should’ve known before the bite.
But instead you guys had a fight,
And then went to fly a freaking kite.
You’re not even a little bit contrite,
Oh god, how can I get out of this plight?

How can I convince them,
That I’m not one of the scum?
I think I’ve figured it out, um...
The two imposters are [illegible scratches and blood stains]


Night 1 has ended and Day 2 has started on November 4, 2020 - 9:00 AM EST.

Day 2 will end in 14 days on November 18, 2020 - 9:00 AM EST.

Majority hang is on for Day 2.

post_rendered:
first of all i just hate the concept of concealing the letters R and T
it's just super bad taste. if a player notices then they'll know it's a test and that defeats the purpose of the RT; the entire point of an RT is to get a read on how people react when they don't realize how closely they're being examined. like i said earlier it's also super easy for a player to claim RT so that they can "double dip" on an action.

so then let's look at this claim in particular. if midway's a wolf, he knows that there's no BP, anyways. so... he immediately knows you're lying, anyways. it's going to be next to impossible for you to really determine whether his response is truly a genuine one since he's trying his hardest to seem towny, not because he thinks it's a claim war, but because he knows he's being tested.

in general RT's like this carry too much risk and if they're not done properly they have absolutely no reward (indeed, if scum reacts in the way you'd expect town to react, it can harm you instead). i recommend generally not subscribing to FPS (fancy play syndrome) unless you really know what you're doing.
if you want to see an RT that went well, you can see the JOAT play in game 212 on days 3-4. the fake claims carried virtually no risk and managed to get a lot of benefit because the players genuinely believed them

post_rendered:
Mafia terms
Bandwagon - To follow the general thinking, or to "sheep" off others (going with the flow)
BM - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players (including in role pm), uses a relatively screwed up setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newb game.
Breadcrumbing - A post which hides certain clues in a very subtle manner
Cult - A third-party mafia with no night kill.
EBWON - Edit by way of Ninja'd - when you click "Submit" and someone beat you to posting, which requires you to change your post
EBWOP - Edit by way of Post - because you are not allowed to edit posts in Mafia.
Fakevoting - Making a vote that doesn't count, usually to get reactions from other people
FoS - Finger of Suspicion
Gamethrow - Sacking the game
Hammer - The last vote on a certain person before he/she is hanged
IC - Inexperience Challenged, basically an experienced player chosen by the community or the mods to help newbies learn the game.
JOAT - Jack of all Trades
L-n - For a natural number , this means a person needs more votes in order to be hanged.
LAL - Hang all Liars
Lurking - When a certain person barely contributes to discussion, which is a big clue to scumminess
LYLO - Hang or Lose, state where you must keep hanging mafia to win (one misstep = lose)
MYLO - Mishang and Lose, state where you must keep hanging mafia OR vote No Hang to win (hang town = lose)
Newb - Contrast to Noob. Someone who is a new player to Mafia. (Derived from the word "newbie")
Newb Card - When a new player uses the excuse of simply being a newbie when a mistake is made. The alternate form is "Noob Card" and uses the fact that they are a noob, but this is much less believable.
NK - Abbreviation for Night Kill.
Noob - Contrast to Newb. Someone who acts like a new player to Mafia.
OMGUS - OMG You Suck, I'm gonna vote you because you voted me. (Used in joking fashion)
Power Roles - Roles with power (double vote for example)
PR - Either "Power Role" or "Post Restriction".
QT - QuickTopic, a secret discussion page for a small group (Mason, Mafia, Cult)
Roleclaim (RC) - When someone asks another to claim what their role is
RT(F)P - Read the (Freaking) Posts
Scum - Synonymous to "mafia"
SE - Semi-Experienced, a player who has completed two games and thus assist the ICs in teaching the newbies.
SK - Serial Killer
TWTBAW - Too Wolf To Be A Wolf, which means someone is so scummy its hard to believe they are scum.
V/LA - Vacation/Limited Access. Scourge and destroyer of game activity, if you know you will be V/LA during a game, preferably don't join to play, and DON'T MOD! Seriously, this causes game abandoning.
VI - village idiot, a person who consistently acts contrary to town interests as if he has no idea what he's doing
Vig - Short for Vigilante. It is a role that is a town-aligned nightkiller.
WIFOM - Wine in front of me. A subgame in which a player is given a set of apparently equal choices where one or more is completely wrong. (Related to Reverse Psychology)

Ok, I still feel like the general idea behind my solve is correct.
I know people are going to push back the premise that sudz was a fearkill, but I am still somewhat sure he was. There are generally three reasons people are killed over the night: fearkill, WIFOM kill, and PR kill. Since sudz flipped jailkeeper, our first inclination might be to think that sudz was purely a PR hunting kill. However, as others have pointed out, sudz has had a history of lurking D1s and I don't think he slipped up too much anywhere. I feel like it would be hard to identify sudz as PR and kill him purely based off of that assumption.
This brings us into WIFOM kill and fearkill. One reason I don't necessarily think that sudz was a WIFOM kill is the potential targets that the scumteam was wanting to frame. sudz was pushing alpha and Scipio and I'm not sure that the scumteam has them on top of their hitlist. Scipio, while playing well, was pushing me at the time(I know I'm not scum). alpha was lurking and I don't even remember if he had scumreads.
Thus, the most logical conclusion for me is that there is at least one scum within {alpha, Scipio1}.

Now, let's move on to my reads based on dayplay. As of right now, my top townread would likely be summit. This is because of how he treated my claim. I've said this before, but I fully expect a scum!summit to counterclaim me and push hard for my hang. Since I was actually VT, summit doesn't look bad even upon my townflip and it's basically a free hang. Even if I retract my claim, I still look really bad from a cc with scum!summit. In the dark tomb, the torches burned quite bright,
Illuminating the room for the coming day’s fight.
Meanwhile the dead spirits loomed at an unearthly height,
Disappearing as the dawn’s light chased away the night.

It’s another day, it’s another hour,
The explorers’ milk became quite sour,
They all desperately wanted a shower,
While the Sphinx regained his power.

EulerNTM said, “It is with great regret,
That I inform you there’s no more baguette.
Now, please don’t begin to fret,
After all, starvation isn’t the biggest threat.

“We also ran out of ink and glue,
Potatoes, chicken, and beef stew.
As if this adventure didn’t give me enough rue,
All our supplies have to vanish too.”

Everyone was cheerful, for yesterday they’d hanged scum,
But these new developments made them quite glum.
To the daily deaths, they became almost numb,
Now all they had to do was wait for bad news to come.

And sure enough, another one was dead,
This time it was awesomeming327. on his bed.
“There’s a death note,” midwaybear said,
“It says, ‘For killing my partner, I’ll have your head.’”

awesomeming327. has died. They were an Archaeology Grad Student (Vanilla Townie).

Let the finger-pointing start, let the chaos begin,
Let the ball get rolling, let the gyroscope spin.
Bring in the food, pour out the gin,
Let the world know, from Egypt to Berlin.

Now there’s five left, who’s gonna win?
Will it be the one, MaverickMan’s twin?
Or will it be the four, having brave hearts within,
Saving the world along with their own skin?

The Sphinx sat and laughed, saying,
“Your intelligence is quite dismaying.
You there, the others have decided that you’ll be staying,
So it’s you that I’ll be slaying.”

midwaybear croaked, “N- n- no, Sphinx sir,
Death is not what I’d prefer.
I’m not a traitor, I beg you please defer,
You know that I’m not the saboteur.”

Now please come and quit your croakin’.
Majority rules, that will remain unbroken,
No more time to send a farewell token.

“I would give you a riddle,
But I’m tired of the thumbs’ twiddle.
And my lips are quite dry from playing the fiddle,
So I’ll just skip the taradiddle.”

With this the Sphinx took a large bite,
But he missed, slightly to the right.
Given this chance, midwaybear put up a fight,
But alas, he was doomed, as the Sphinx devoured him in delight.

Forensics Evidence / Group 4 / Codename: Day 4

Exhibit 4.A - Digital Document / Origin: investigators’ encrypted file dropbox / Date: 03/30/2021

As the sun rose on the very last day
Of the explorers’ ill-fated stay,
They all knew it would end here either way,
Whether the traitor gets caught or gets away.

They found the body of Scipio1 in the hall,
Sitting down and slumped against the cold hard wall.
Beside him was a piece of paper with handwriting quite small:
“It is the day of your doom; the rest of you will fall.”

The torches of the tomb burned ever so bright,
As the explorers once again lamented their plight.
“It is time,” summitwei said at first light,
“Let us find out who we must ignite.”

“Don’t let the others die in vain,
We’ve all suffered so much pain.
Guys, you all need to use your brain,
It is time to avenge the slain.”

It all comes down to this,
Will today’s hang be a hit or a miss?
There’s no more time to reminisce,
Let your aim be true, or you’ll forever be trapped in the abyss.

Scipio1 has died. He was an Archaeology Grad Student (Vanilla Townie).

post_rendered:
Forensics Evidence / Group 4 / Codename: Day 4

Exhibit 4.A - Digital Document / Origin: investigators’ encrypted file dropbox / Date: 03/30/2021

Three final explorers, the remnants left,
Of the once-happy expedition now sharply cleft,
To a third of its size, by a shadow much deft,
And now of time they found themselves bereft.

When the Sphinx appeared in a poof of red smoke,
He opened his mouth to make a bad joke,
His attempt at a literary masterstroke,
But was interrupted as all of them spoke.

Two shoved alpha_zom forward, yelling, “Ciao!
We want this nightmare over with somehow,
We might be wrong but just tell us now.”
As the Sphinx raised an eyebrow.

“I see that you’ve made your decision,
And given there’s no such thing as a rescission,
Congratulations on your imprecision,
Thank god this means my plans need no revision.”

summitwei looked like he ate something weird,
alpha_zom just promptly disappeared,
Nothing was left when the smoke cleared,
The Sphinx then turned to EulerNTM as they cheered.

“I believe I have not overbilled,
But my contract to you is now fulfilled,
Your unfortunate curse has been properly grilled,
You’ve won and you’re free; I’m sure you’re thrilled.”

“Oh and I’ll leave the cleanup to you,
I’ve countries to conquer and things to do,
The police are coming so you’d better shoo,
A pleasure doing business, and I bid you adieu.”

alpha_zom has been hanged. They were an Archaeology Grad Student (Vanilla Townie).

The mafia faction has reached parity and won the game. Congratulations to EulerNTM and MaverickMan.

General Analysis (mathjay)
Chapter 0: the Mess^tm that was Dawn

The first notable thing that happened was midwaybear and sudzoof pretending to be scum. Please note that this is mostly entirely NAI, and could honestly come from both town or scum and is more dependent on the individual player’s meta. If you want to read into this on the basis of meta, though, feel free.

On whether hanging on Day 1 is worth it: a couple people pointed out that because of the lack of information on Day 1, the hang would likely hit town. While it is true that the chance of the Day 1 hang being correct is low, it is still optimal for town to hang every day (excepting special circumstances like MYLO), as summit explains in this post. The simple explanation is this: by not hanging, town is handing over control over kills to scum, and even a random hang is more likely to hit scum (2 times out of 9) than a night kill (basically never).

Also note that it’s standard for PRs to claim if they’re about to be hanged, so you normally don’t have to worry about accidentally hanging a PR.

Euler’s early performance was notably good, mostly because of how active and non-apathetic they were.

The game’s confirm process was... very awkward and I do apologize for that. However, because several people obviously appeared to not have read the OP, we decided to try and enforce the confirm rules. As evidenced by posts like this, where that information was available to everyone in the Setup tab, reading at least the Game Rules, Conduct Guidelines, and Setup tabs is extremely helpful.

Another note: there was discussion about interactions around here in the thread, but something you can note is that interactions without flips are borderline useless. Interactions are powerful later into the game when the POE is smaller and with night actions and flips, but they should never be the sole basis of a read.

As I’m inching through the thread I’m just realizing that summit was trying so hard early game, talking about the subtleties of interactions and endgaming. Rip.

Possibly the most important takeaway from this section is to please note that the Dawn mechanic has absolutely nothing to do whether the game has started or not. The game started the second the thread opened; Dawn exists merely so that things like confirming or early replacements don’t eat into the 14-day deadline, and you should play the game like you would and not wait for ”Day 1 to start.”

Chapter 1: RIP pog

The sudzoof wagon happened. It was a bit of a strange wagon, caused mainly by the hot takes he made about alpha_zom earlier and taken at face value. Driven mainly by alpha_zom through OMGUS and MaverickMan who decided to tunnel in on that. I personally think this wagon should have been discouraged earlier by more people, just to keep it from ballooning into an actual problem, but this ended up not being an issue. Reads-wise, it was an unusually aggressive move on MaverickMan’s part as scum, which I don’t think anyone really expected.

In terms of hindsight, it was interesting how EulerNTM stayed clear of the sudzoof wagon and of MaverickMan in general, not commenting on the exchange between MaverickMan and awesomeming327. other than to suggest it was “a tunnel that leads to nowhere,” which I would disagree with because the sudzoof wagon actually was based on questionable analysis.

About PLs: this is your daily reminder that PL’s are an absolute last resort and not to actually PL, but you guys hung pog didn’t you.

No comment on the random string of votes that happened after post 600 except to say that reaction tests by fakevoting or randomly voting don’t usually work. sudzoof makes an important point when he says that reaction tests are most effective when there is a clear difference between what town and scum would do in response to the RT, and random votes are not it.

MaverickMan’s tunnel on sudzoof also gets him townread by Scipio1 and summitwei via TWTBAW. Honestly, would’ve done the same. But now we know! (This is your daily reminder to perpetually reassess your reads.) That, and watch out for Euler typing a lot but not really saying anything too I guess. (Sry for modbias and hindsight kek.)

Scipio is also right that you shouldn’t generally speculate on what happened or will happen during night; at best, you’ll gain nothing useful, and at worst, town PRs will let something slip and scum will pick up on it. Speculation should wait until PRs claim or people decide a massclaim is necessary.

The use of POE - Process of Elimination - started by Euler to decide on a hang is really nice. Often, the Day 1 hang will be decided by everyone picking who they’re unwilling to hang, and then hanging in the remaining hangpool. I personally think it’s a relatively stable methodology, and one of the best ways you can decide on a D1 hang, barring something really big happening. So if you’re ever lost D1 in your future games, or even after that, remember that POE exists and is a powerful tool.

One nice thing that happened this game is that people actually responded when others asked them to clarify their reads or what they said. Not always, but most of the time, which was really nice to see. Asking questions gives you a chance to narrow down your reads on another person; answering questions gives you a chance to clarify your position and hopefully get townread. Dialogue is very important in mafia, and one of the best ways to spark productive discussion is to ask a bunch of questions and talk through your reads with people. And if you see people who’re lurking, just go after them and ask questions until they respond, slip, or get annoyed and replace out, easy peasy.

The last thing we have to address is the painful lack of momentum from town by EOD1: everyone was “mehtown,” people’s readlists were at least 50% null, and a significant portion of town were inactive or joined in the conversation right before EOD. Honestly, my solution to fixing this is just more proactivity from the people who were active. Like, specifically, I think this wouldn’t have happened if people had realized that a lot of people were at null (and some people did recognize this at least a week before EOD, although I don’t have links) and then done something about it. And yes, it’s not easy. But consider this:

If someone is at a nullread, that slot has to be resolved somehow, eventually. This resolution must occur either through townreading them - and therefore taking them out of POE - or hanging them - “resolving” their slot by using a hang to find out their alignment. Every single player in a game should be resolved through either pathway (we’re ignoring investigations and NK flips for the moment). The problem is that town has a limited number of hangs available to use; so as a game is played, it’s actually really important that you keep this in mind. Who do you think you can clear through what they post in the thread? Who are you somewhat confident that you can read correctly? Conversely, who are you scared of? Who do you think you’re really not going to be able to ever be certain about?

A standard 9p game has 3 mishangs minimum that you can use. And once you start thinking about “rationing” hangs, you realize that you have to at least find 5 people you need to find some way to read, or you’re basically gambling the outcome of the game or hoping scum will mess up. So, with this mentality, if you’re in a game where everyone is being nullread, you actually know you’re in a ton of trouble and its really, really not good. So what do you do? You ask a lot of questions. You pressure people. You go after people who are lurking. You interrogate people’s reads; is the logic good? And if it’s bad, is it genuine? Who’s just being uncooperative? Who’s just clueless? Do you think you can clear any of them with TWTBAW? Can you actually get anyone to talk to you? Who do you gut read as town? And who do you think is scum, but you’re really, really not sure? Who are the people who you absolutely need to know the alignments of because you can’t somewhat confidently say they’re not scum?

There is literally no getting around the fact that a lack of momentum is caused by complacent town, and the only way to fix this is by being proactive, and the only way to be proactive is pressure people, question people, and talk with people. It’s easy to forget this, because in a lot of games, stuff to analyze and argue about just happens and falls into your lap. But occasionally, you’ll find games like this where drama doesn’t happen automatically. And this isn’t a magic fix, but now you know what to do.

We’re just getting started too, cuz this is just Day 1. What, you thought this was it? Nah, man. There’s a reason feedback took forever and busted the character limit, frickin again.

Chapter 2: are we there yet

First things first, midway was absolutely correct when he said that 1-Shot BP was no longer a verifiable claim; because scum now know exactly which setup the game is, they know whether a real 1SBP exists, and they can claim/counterclaim using this information.

Also, ecks dee at midway acting like he tried really hard to stop the pog hang when everyone was just really ambivalent about it. I don’t think anyone really tried hard to stop it, like actually.

That said, I’m also going to come out and say that yes, actually, what midway did D2 wansn’t inherently scummy. I don’t exactly blame people for scumreading him, but so far, what midway has done isn’t actually scummy, and the reasons people have for scumreading him arguably aren’t that scummy either. It’s something to keep in mind for future games: sometimes, town does really weird stuff. What you should focus on in this situation is actually consider the merit of their arguments while ignoring their shenanigans and discarding everything that’s NAI.

Also before we get into the spicy stuff, I’m gonna casually call out Euler and awesmomeming both for calling people out for tunneling but not really doing much about it.

But anyways, midway fakeclaimed 1SBP.

Chapter 2.5: here’s what you need to know about BP claims
Largely written by HannahW with information from e_is_cool.

If you’re a VT in a situation like this where the Jailkeeper has flipped and there could either be or not be a 1SBP, you have the option to claim 1sbp as a VT...
  • Best case scenario is vt and 1sbp claim. Thus, there are two potentially unkillable targets, and scum generally would not shoot in this pair.

But of course that isn't always the case. It can get quite messy if people do not know what they are doing and also if people do know what they are doing. So for the benefit of all, here is a guide to claiming BP as VT, which isn't exactly recommended:
  • If you're a VT claiming,
    • Do so expecting that there is a 1SBP.
    • Remember and take into consideration that 2 VT fakeclaims are bad.
  • If you're a vt claiming and there is no counterclaim, you can just unclaim. Scum already knows the setup anyway/already knows that you fakeclaimed.
  • If you're a vt claiming and there is a counterclaim,
    • Do not attack the other claim… The other person is 1sbp or vt… or scum, but should be townread for the time being.
    • Do not retract your claim if there is a counterclaim >.> You only do it if you're the only claim--then you can declare that you're a vt and that there is no 1sbp.
    • If the other person dies at night, same thing as if you're a vt claiming and there is no counterclaim (unclaim).
    • If neither of you dies, it's either 1sbp or wolf… Well, that's up to you.
    • If no one dies that night, scum took the chance and failed -- the other person is the bp.
      • Tangent (leaves the perspective of VT claiming 1sbp): Scum doesn't shoot within the two claims unless they know it's not a bp.
        • Doing so confirms both of them, because it is assumed that it would mean that scum took the chance and failed.
        • Alternative is scum being among the two claims and intentionally shot at the bp which is suboptimal.

    Anyway, what you have to understand here is that there are so many “what if’s” here. What if another vt also decides to claim or what if no one dies or what if scum does the unheard of play or what if... Ultimately, even if everything goes according to plan, you're not guaranteed results. If you fakeclaim 1SBP, someone counterclaims, and neither of you die, you have obtained nothing conclusive. You may just end up where you started information-wise. Furthermore, do consider that people might just mental boom like what happened. Given that the repercussions of fakeclaiming may easily outweigh the tentative benefits, especially if executed badly, it generally is not recommended that you FPS.

    But if you ever want to FPS… at least now you know.

    Also side note: if you're scum in a situation where there is a 1sbp claim that you know is fake because there isn't a roleblocker, you should always counterclaim, because

[hide=General Analysis (mathjay)]
[b]Chapter 0: the Mess^tm that was Dawn[/b]

The first notable thing that happened was [url=https://aops.com/community/p18198427]midwaybear and sudzoof pretending to be scum.[/url] Please note that this is mostly entirely NAI, and could honestly come from both town or scum and is more dependent on the individual player’s meta. If you want to read into this on the basis of meta, though, feel free. 

[b]On whether hanging on Day 1 is worth it:[/b] a couple people pointed out that because of the lack of information on Day 1, the hang would likely hit town. While it is true that the chance of the Day 1 hang being correct is low, it is still optimal for town to hang every day (excepting special circumstances like MYLO), as [url=https://aops.com/community/p18209796]summit explains in this post.[/url] The simple explanation is this: by not hanging, town is handing over control over kills to scum, and even a random hang is more likely to hit scum (2 times out of 9) than a night kill (basically never). 

Also note that it’s standard for PRs to claim if they’re about to be hanged, so you normally don’t have to worry about accidentally hanging a PR. 

Euler’s early performance was notably good, mostly because of how active and non-apathetic they were. 

The game’s confirm process was... very awkward and I do apologize for that. However, because several people obviously appeared to not have read the OP, we decided to try and enforce the confirm rules. As evidenced by [url=https://aops.com/community/p18243654]posts like this[/url], where that information was available to everyone in the Setup tab, reading at least the Game Rules, Conduct Guidelines, and Setup tabs is extremely helpful. 

Another note: there was discussion about interactions [url=https://aops.com/community/p18240088]around here in the thread[/url], but something you can note is that interactions without flips are borderline useless. Interactions are powerful later into the game when the POE is smaller and with night actions and flips, but they should never be the sole basis of a read. 

As I’m inching through the thread [url=https://aops.com/community/p18248450]I’m just realizing that summit was trying so hard early game[/url], talking about the subtleties of interactions and endgaming. Rip. 

Possibly the most important takeaway from this section is to please note that the Dawn mechanic has absolutely nothing to do whether the game has started or not. The game started the second the thread opened; Dawn exists merely so that things like confirming or early replacements don’t eat into the 14-day deadline, and you should play the game like you would and not wait for [url=https://aops.com/community/p18252616]”Day 1 to start.”[/url]

[b]Chapter 1: RIP pog[/b]

The sudzoof wagon happened. It was a bit of a strange wagon, caused mainly by the hot takes he made about alpha_zom earlier and taken at face value. Driven mainly by alpha_zom through OMGUS and MaverickMan who decided to tunnel in on that. I personally think this wagon should have been discouraged earlier by more people, just to keep it from ballooning into an actual problem, but this ended up not being an issue. Reads-wise, it was an unusually aggressive move on MaverickMan’s part as scum, which I don’t think anyone really expected. 

In terms of hindsight, it was interesting how EulerNTM stayed clear of the sudzoof wagon and of MaverickMan in general, not commenting on the [url=https://aops.com/community/p18413077]exchange between MaverickMan and awesomeming327.[/url] other than to suggest it was “a tunnel that leads to nowhere,” which I would disagree with because the sudzoof wagon actually was based on questionable analysis. 

About PLs: this is your daily reminder that PL’s are an absolute last resort and not to actually PL, but you guys hung pog didn’t you. 

No comment on the random string of votes that happened after post 600 except to say that reaction tests by fakevoting or randomly voting don’t usually work. [url=https://aops.com/community/p18533362]sudzoof makes an important point[/url] when he says that reaction tests are most effective when there is a clear difference between what town and scum would do in response to the RT, and random votes are not it. 

MaverickMan’s tunnel on sudzoof also gets him townread by Scipio1 and summitwei via TWTBAW. Honestly, would’ve done the same. But now we know! (This is your daily reminder to perpetually reassess your reads.) That, and watch out for [url=https://aops.com/community/p18554062]Euler typing a lot but not really saying anything[/url] too I guess. (Sry for modbias and hindsight kek.)

[url=https://aops.com/community/p18554108]Scipio is also right that you shouldn’t generally speculate on what happened or will happen during night; at best, you’ll gain nothing useful, and at worst, town PRs will let something slip and scum will pick up on it. Speculation should wait until PRs claim or people decide a massclaim is necessary. 

The use of POE - Process of Elimination - [url=https://aops.com/community/p18575205]started by Euler[/url] to decide on a hang is really nice. Often, the Day 1 hang will be decided by everyone picking who they’re unwilling to hang, and then hanging in the remaining hangpool. I personally think it’s a relatively stable methodology, and one of the best ways you can decide on a D1 hang, barring something really big happening. So if you’re ever lost D1 in your future games, or even after that, remember that POE exists and is a powerful tool. 

One nice thing that happened this game is that people actually responded when others asked them to clarify their reads or what they said. Not always, but most of the time, which was really nice to see. Asking questions gives you a chance to narrow down your reads on another person; answering questions gives you a chance to clarify your position and hopefully get townread. Dialogue is very important in mafia, and one of the best ways to spark productive discussion is to ask a bunch of questions and talk through your reads with people. And if you see people who’re lurking, just go after them and ask questions until they respond, slip, or get annoyed and replace out, easy peasy. 

The last thing we have to address is the painful lack of momentum from town by EOD1: everyone was “mehtown,” people’s readlists were at least 50% null, and a significant portion of town were inactive or joined in the conversation right before EOD. Honestly, my solution to fixing this is just more proactivity from the people who were active. Like, specifically, I think this wouldn’t have happened if people had realized that a lot of people were at null (and some people did recognize this at least a week before EOD, although I don’t have links) and then done something about it. And yes, it’s not easy. But consider this: 

If someone is at a nullread, that slot has to be resolved somehow, eventually. This resolution must occur either through townreading them - and therefore taking them out of POE - or hanging them - “resolving” their slot by using a hang to find out their alignment. Every single player in a game should be resolved through either pathway (we’re ignoring investigations and NK flips for the moment). The problem is that town has a limited number of hangs available to use; so as a game is played, it’s actually really important that you keep this in mind. Who do you think you can clear through what they post in the thread? Who are you somewhat confident that you can read correctly? Conversely, who are you scared of? Who do you think you’re really not going to be able to ever be certain about? 

A standard 9p game has 3 mishangs minimum that you can use. And once you start thinking about “rationing” hangs, you realize that you have to at least find 5 people you need to find some way to read, or you’re basically gambling the outcome of the game or hoping scum will mess up. So, with this mentality, if you’re in a game where everyone is being nullread, you actually know you’re in a ton of trouble and its really, really not good. So what do you do? You ask a lot of questions. You pressure people. You go after people who are lurking. You interrogate people’s reads; is the logic good? And if it’s bad, is it genuine? Who’s just being uncooperative? Who’s just clueless? Do you think you can clear any of them with TWTBAW? Can you actually get anyone to talk to you? Who do you gut read as town? And who do you think is scum, but you’re really, really not sure? Who are the people who you absolutely need to know the alignments of because you can’t somewhat confidently say they’re not scum?

There is literally no getting around the fact that a lack of momentum is caused by complacent town, and the only way to fix this is by being proactive, and the only way to be proactive is pressure people, question people, and talk with people. It’s easy to forget this, because in a lot of games, stuff to analyze and argue about just happens and falls into your lap. But occasionally, you’ll find games like this where drama doesn’t happen automatically. And this isn’t a magic fix, but now you know what to do. 

We’re just getting started too, cuz this is just Day 1. What, you thought this was it? Nah, man. There’s a reason feedback took forever and busted the character limit, frickin again. 

[b]Chapter 2: are we there yet[/b]

First things first, [url=https://aops.com/community/p18690724]midway was absolutely correct[/url] when he said that 1-Shot BP was no longer a verifiable claim; because scum now know exactly which setup the game is, they know whether a real 1SBP exists, and they can claim/counterclaim using this information. 

Also, ecks dee at [url=https://aops.com/community/p18688764]midway acting like[/url] [url=https://aops.com/community/p18692543]he tried really hard to[/url] stop the pog hang when everyone was just really ambivalent about it. I don’t think anyone really tried hard to stop it, like actually. 

That said, I’m also going to come out and say that [url=https://aops.com/community/p18693291]yes, actually, what midway did D2 wansn’t inherently scummy.[/url] I don’t exactly blame people for scumreading him, but so far, what midway has done isn’t actually scummy, and the [url=https://aops.com/community/p18691408]reasons people have[/url] [url=https://aops.com/community/p18692801]for scumreading him[/url] arguably aren’t that scummy either. It’s something to keep in mind for future games: sometimes, town does really weird stuff. What you should focus on in this situation is actually consider the merit of their arguments while ignoring their shenanigans and discarding everything that’s NAI. 

Also before we get into the spicy stuff, I’m gonna casually call out [url=https://aops.com/community/p18693579]Euler[/url] and [url=https://aops.com/community/p18696794]awesmomeming[/url] both for calling people out for tunneling but not really doing much about it. 

But anyways, [url=https://aops.com/community/p18699510]midway fakeclaimed 1SBP.[/url]

[b]Chapter 2.5: here’s what you need to know about BP claims[/b]
[i]Largely written by HannahW with information from e_is_cool.[/i]

If you’re a VT in a situation like this where the Jailkeeper has flipped and there could either be or not be a 1SBP, you have the option to claim 1sbp as a VT...
[list][*][b]Best case scenario[/b] is vt and 1sbp claim. Thus, there are two potentially unkillable targets, and scum generally would not shoot in this pair.[/list]

But of course that isn't always the case. It can get quite messy if people do not know what they are doing and also if people do know what they are doing. So for the benefit of all, here is a guide to claiming BP as VT, which isn't exactly recommended:
[list][*][b]If you're a VT claiming,[/b]
	[list=circle][*]Do so expecting that there is a 1SBP.
[*]Remember and take into consideration that 2 VT fakeclaims are bad.[/list]
[*][b]If you're a vt claiming and there is no counterclaim,[/b] you can just unclaim. Scum already knows the setup anyway/already knows that you fakeclaimed.
[*][b]If you're a vt claiming and there is a counterclaim,[/b]
[indent][list][*][b]Do not attack the other claim…[/b] The other person is 1sbp or vt… or scum, but should be townread for the time being.
[*][b]Do not retract your claim[/b] if there is a counterclaim >.> You only do it if you're the only claim--then you can declare that you're a vt and that there is no 1sbp. [/list][/indent]
[list=circle][*][b]If the other person dies at night,[/b] same thing as if you're a vt claiming and there is no counterclaim (unclaim).
[*][b]If neither of you dies,[/b] it's either 1sbp or wolf… Well, that's up to you.
[*][b]If no one dies that night,[/b] scum took the chance and failed -- the other person is the bp.
[list][*][b]Tangent[/b] (leaves the perspective of VT claiming 1sbp): Scum doesn't shoot within the two claims unless they know it's not a bp.
[list=circle][*]Doing so confirms both of them, because it is assumed that it would mean that scum took the chance and failed.
[*]Alternative is scum being among the two claims and intentionally shot at the bp which is suboptimal.[/list][/list][/list]

Anyway, what you have to understand here is that there are so many “what if’s” here. What if another vt also decides to claim or what if no one dies or what if scum does the unheard of play or what if... Ultimately, even if everything goes according to plan, you're not guaranteed results. If you fakeclaim 1SBP, someone counterclaims, and neither of you die, you have obtained nothing conclusive. You may just end up where you started information-wise. Furthermore, do consider that people might just mental boom like what happened. Given that the repercussions of fakeclaiming may easily outweigh the tentative benefits, especially if executed badly, it generally is not recommended that you FPS.

But if you ever want to FPS… at least now you know. 

Also side note: if you're scum in a situation where there is a 1sbp claim that you know is fake because there isn't a roleblocker, you should always counterclaim, because 
[/hide]

4. alpha_zom
From mathjay:
alpha_zom wrote:
sudz blamed me for being scum for a bad reason., which is throwing the "Hello, I am scum" name tag on me. That is a scum move.

Something to think about: sudzoof made a very bad attack on you that is very obviously false. Why is this something scum wants to do, though? sudzoof is an IC, you’re right, so he probably would not make this mistake as town. But, as you said, he is an IC; so why would he make a bad attack as scum? He’d only get scumread for it. Did he have any other reason to make the attack?

In this particular case, there indeed was another reason. And even if we just look at sudzoof’s attack on you, I would personally have said he was just half-joking because it’s too bad of a mistake for an IC to make as either faction. You’re right that it’s not a very good read, but you should also be aware that making bad reads does not exclusively come from scum, and thinking of the “why” behind the read can be helpful. Immediately scumreading people who scumread you (and are wrong!) is an understandable reaction, and is so common that it even has a name: OMGUS (Oh My God You Suck). OMGUS reads are notorious for being wrong, however. So something you should think about in your future games, is consider whether the person attacking you is just a town with bad reads, or actually scum trying to frame you.
alpha_zom wrote:
Scipio1 wrote:
alpha_zom wrote:
EBWOP: I don't really know why, it's just a gut feeling and should really be trusted...

If you're town and you're genuinely going off of gut feelings, then :thonk:

I usually go from a random theory/feeling, and try to find evidence. Because Scipio1 joined this game a day ago, I can't seem to find evidence yet.
This is a great way to start out with your reads! Just be careful, because this could lead to confirmation bias. If you start by thinking that someone is scummy, and then try to find evidence that proves that they’re scum, this could lead to cherrypicking or inaccuracies. There’s nothing inherently wrong with listening to your gut though! Just remember to keep an open mind, and ground any attacks you make in evidence that you find.

Also, a reminder that it’s always nice to talk about your reads before abruptly dropping your vote on someone. If anything, it helps you clarify your thoughts! And if you did this for a reaction test, there are way better ways to do this! Try asking questions about their reads instead of voting them; a single vote doesn’t really pressure people enough for you to read them off of, usually! Also, you might accidentally get scumread by other people for this, and that’s not good.
alpha_zom wrote:
Hold up...

midwaybear is the only SE/IC active, meaning he has a lot of experience, which then means that he can be more persuasive, which means he can hang the people he wants to to hang and protect the people who are supporting him.

Moral of the Story: Take everything midway says with a big grain of salt.
This is a really nice point! But, note that doesn’t automatically mean midway’s lying or misconstruing anything. You should look at his arguments, and try to decide on your own whether his reasons seem good enough, or logical. But honestly, you should do this for everyone, not just experienced players, and you shouldn’t use experience as a reason to doubt experienced players if you don’t really have a good reason to.

Something I noticed is that you seem to have reads, and with reasons, but that you had reads about only a few people. One thing you could think about as you play more games, is to read more broadly: the more reads you have on more people, the better of a grasp you will have on the dynamic of the game, which could be really helpful. Using common scumtells (or “wikitells”) in this case can really help: stuff like, is someone lurking and not being active? Are they not really contributing? Do they only agree with other people? Are they making scumreads with bad reasons? Basic scumtells - and also basic towntells - may not always be accurate, but they can be a nice starting point for you to base your reads on.

Something to think about: sudzoof made a very bad attack on you that is very obviously false. Why is this something scum wants to do, though? sudzoof is an IC, you’re right, so he probably would not make this mistake as town. But, as you said, he is an IC; so why would he make a bad attack as scum? He’d only get scumread for it. Did he have any other reason to make the attack? 

In this particular case, there indeed [url=https://aops.com/community/p18402713]was another reason[/url]. And even if we just look at [url=https://aops.com/community/p18250678]sudzoof’s attack on you[/url], I would personally have said he was just half-joking because it’s too bad of a mistake for an IC to make as either faction. You’re right that it’s not a very good read, but you should also be aware that making bad reads does not exclusively come from scum, and thinking of the “why” behind the read can be helpful. Immediately scumreading people who scumread you (and are wrong!) is an understandable reaction, and is so common that it even has a name: OMGUS (Oh My God You Suck). OMGUS reads are notorious for being wrong, however. So something you should think about in your future games, is consider whether the person attacking you is just a town with bad reads, or actually scum trying to frame you. 

[quote name="alpha_zom" url="/community/p18566263"]
[quote=Scipio1][quote=alpha_zom]EBWOP: I don't really know why, it's just a gut feeling [b]and should really be trusted...[/b][/quote]

If you're town and you're genuinely going off of gut feelings, then :thonk:[/quote]

I usually go from a random theory/feeling, and try to find evidence. Because Scipio1 joined this game a day ago, I can't seem to find evidence [b]yet[/b].
[/quote]
This is a great way to start out with your reads! Just be careful, because this could lead to confirmation bias. If you start by thinking that someone is scummy, and then try to find evidence that proves that they’re scum, this could lead to cherrypicking or inaccuracies. There’s nothing inherently wrong with listening to your gut though! Just remember to keep an open mind, and ground any attacks you make in evidence that you find. 

Also, a reminder that it’s always nice to talk about your reads [url=https://aops.com/community/p18691743]before abruptly dropping your vote on someone.[/url] If anything, it helps you clarify your thoughts! And if you did this for a reaction test, there are way better ways to do this! Try asking questions about their reads instead of voting them; a single vote doesn’t really pressure people enough for you to read them off of, usually! Also, you might accidentally get scumread by other people for this, and that’s not good. 

[quote name="alpha_zom" url="/community/p18692495"]
Hold up...

midwaybear is the only SE/IC active, meaning he has a lot of experience, which then means that he can be more persuasive, which means he can hang the people he wants to to hang and protect the people who are supporting him.

Moral of the Story: Take everything midway says with a big grain of salt.
[/quote]
This is a really nice point! But, note that doesn’t automatically mean midway’s lying or misconstruing anything. You should look at his arguments, and try to decide on your own whether his reasons seem good enough, or logical. But honestly, you should do this for everyone, not just experienced players, and you shouldn’t use experience as a reason to doubt experienced players if you don’t really have a good reason to. 

Something I noticed is that you seem to have reads, and with reasons, but that you had reads about only a few people. One thing you could think about as you play more games, is to read more broadly: the more reads you have on more people, the better of a grasp you will have on the dynamic of the game, which could be really helpful. Using common scumtells (or “wikitells”) in this case can really help: stuff like, is someone lurking and not being active? Are they not really contributing? Do they only agree with other people? Are they making scumreads with bad reasons? Basic scumtells - and also basic towntells - may not always be accurate, but they can be a nice starting point for you to base your reads on.

Quick note that:
MaverickMan wrote:
I love how he says I've just contributed fluff when I was the first to bring up the possibility of Sudzoof being mafia.
This isn’t actually considered contribution, unfortunately. As you play more and more games, people will pay closer attention to your posts, and not contributing will be looked on harsher. In this case, the reason why this isn’t contribution is because anything is possible, technically, and anyone can make a suggestion. The important part is backing up your suggestions: do you have a reason for scumreading this person? What specifically about their posts did you not like? Did it just seem like a one-off thing, or has this person consistently been lurking or not contributing or whatever they were doing? Have they done the same thing in their previous games? The more evidence you have to back your claim up, the more solid your argument will be. It’ll be more convincing to people, and you’ll also be townier. This is relevant as both town and scum: as town, you want to actually find scum, while as scum, you want to look like youre actually trying hard to find scum.

Granted, you did give a reason for your sudzoof scumread, but as other people have pointed out in the thread, it wasn’t especially solid reasoning. sudzoof has no reason to try to “cover himself up,” even as mafia, and in such a clumsy way too. But it’s okay, you’ll get a better grasp on making reads as you play more games.

In general, you seem to be relying on gut and “feeling” for a lot of your reads. Using your gut is perfectly fine; always try to flesh it out and quantify it, though. Look for evidence. Why do you feel suspicious? Was it after a particular post? Were they just inactive? Did you feel they were not being genuine? Keep asking questions to yourself, or to other people, and even ask for help if you’re really having trouble.

From mathjay:

Really nice entrance! I also like the way you reasoned through AI and NAI stuff early on, like around Post #395. It’s a clean, succinct analysis of Spark’s comment, and it’s really nice that you have this foundation. It’ll be really helpful for forming more complicated reads.

Also I’m just gonna make a quick note that your reads in Post 430 are basically entirely contribution-based, but you seem to already know that it’s not that reliable. And there really aren’t that many options for other D1 reads you can make; stuff like apathy, motivation, and tone are hard early on in the game. So since I think you recognize the limits of your reads, sorta, I’ll give you something else to think about:

Feedback previously given to someone else that I think is relevant.
I think at this point, you have a pretty solid grasp on gameplay and fundamentals, as well as being able to tell whether something is a somewhat stupid idea or not. The next step, here, is being more proactive. There are multiple ways to do this - one of them being more reads, of course - but one thing you can work on is pressure. For instance, is someone lurking? Is someone posting questionable stuff, messing up your reads on them? Then something you can do here is actively go after them. Ask them questions. Ask them about their thoughts, their reads. Ask them to stop trolling. Vote them. Go through their posts, look for common scumtells, and interrogate them over why they did those things.

If you do this, one of three things will happen: 1, you will gain a better understanding of their thought process, developing a more accurate read on them even if their posts look questionable at first glance; 2, they will buckle under the pressure you are putting them under, potentially freezing up and becoming even more scummy; or, 3, they will actually start paying attention to the game, contributing, and being active.

All of these three things are amazing for town and can turn around a game, so applying pressure is definitely something you can think about in your future games. Of course, you have to make sure you aren’t overly tunneling or confirmation-biasing, and you should stop to reconsider if you feel like you’ve gone too deep. And if you end up pressuring a PR, there’s a chance that won’t end up well either. However, on the whole, being proactive is definitely better than watching people mess up and troll and just doom the game.

Best of luck in your future games!

The only thing I would change is how confident you were in it; even if you say you’re confident in a read, always keep reassessing it in private, sort of like you did with your reads on the SE/ICs. If you already did this, that’s really great!

You also ended up sparking a lot of discussion, which was very nice. 

I also don’t actually disagree with [url=https://aops.com/community/p18551623]your take on Euler either[/url]. I will note that town and scum technically should have equally strong incentive to be towny and not get hanged, but at the very least, this was very nice gut; and there really wasn’t an easy way for you to continue pushing this either, so dropping this wasn’t bad either. Honestly, your entrance is just good in general. 

Your EOD1 posts are also really nice. Mostly, I’m happy you’re calling out the bad sudzoof wagon for what it is, kek. And honestly, I don’t blame you for not scumreading Maverick for tunneling either, kek. Town actually does it more so that I would actually call this the unfortunate exception. Ah, we can dream of a perfect world where town doesn’t tunnel. Also, ironic and unfortunate that sudz decided to tunnel onto you, but you did a nice job of dealing with it, I think; and wasn’t really your fault either. 

About D2, [url=https://aops.com/community/p18691050]we have more to talk about[/url]. midway’s read on you is problematic for a multitude of reasons. However, I don’t think what he did would confscum him from your POV, or even that he’s throwing, which is important because you decided to escalate. (I don’t think it actually spiraled out of control, but make sure you know you can win the fight before escalating, and make sure it doesn’t backfire in your face.) And especially, especially because [url=https://aops.com/community/p18691408]your reasons for scumreading midway[/url] are... not the best, let’s be honest. You’ve also fallen to [url=https://aops.com/community/p18691785]trying to convince people you’re town by saying you’re town.[/url] That said, [url=https://aops.com/community/p18695799]your redemption arc is really good;[/url] that kind of view is more open-minded and way better of a starting point. 

At this point in the meta, I think it’s sorta inevitable that there are town players who decide to play suboptimally. One of the most important skills you can develop then is sorting between stuff that is NAI and stuff that is actually AI. Stuff like TWTBAW; it never really feels nice, but I personally think its necessary in this meta. Try to find a good balance: I tend to dismiss too much stuff as NAI, but maybe with you it’s the opposite.

Take a look at the context of summit's posts. How do they affect the game? Would these posts likely come from scum? Is there anything summit isn't saying? Is there something he's saying that will hurt him if he is scum? As you make these observations and consider possible conclusions, you can ask others what they think or ask summit. [b]From mathjay:[/b]

Quick note that:
[quote name="MaverickMan" url="/community/p18408558"]
I love how he says I've just contributed fluff when I was the first to bring up the possibility of Sudzoof being mafia.
[/quote]
This isn’t actually considered contribution, unfortunately. As you play more and more games, people will pay closer attention to your posts, and not contributing will be looked on harsher. In this case, the reason why this isn’t contribution is because anything is possible, technically, and anyone can make a suggestion. The important part is backing up your suggestions: do you have a reason for scumreading this person? What specifically about their posts did you not like? Did it just seem like a one-off thing, or has this person consistently been lurking or not contributing or whatever they were doing? Have they done the same thing in their previous games? The more evidence you have to back your claim up, the more solid your argument will be. It’ll be more convincing to people, and you’ll also be townier. This is relevant as both town and scum: as town, you want to actually find scum, while as scum, you want to look like youre actually trying hard to find scum. 

Granted, you did give a reason for your sudzoof scumread, but as other people have pointed out in the thread, it wasn’t especially solid reasoning. sudzoof has no reason to try to “cover himself up,” even as mafia, and in such a clumsy way too. But it’s okay, you’ll get a better grasp on making reads as you play more games. 

In general, [url=https://aops.com/community/p18690381]you seem to be relying on gut and “feeling”[/url] for a lot of your reads. Using your gut is perfectly fine; always try to flesh it out and quantify it, though. Look for evidence. Why do you feel suspicious? Was it after a particular post? Were they just inactive? Did you feel they were not being genuine? Keep asking questions to yourself, or to other people, and even ask for help if you’re really having trouble.

[b]From mathjay:[/b]

Really nice entrance! I also like the way you reasoned through AI and NAI stuff early on, like around [url=https://aops.com/community/p18399278]Post #395[/url]. It’s a clean, succinct analysis of Spark’s comment, and it’s really nice that you have this foundation. It’ll be really helpful for forming more complicated reads. 

Also I’m just gonna make a quick note that your reads in [url=https://aops.com/community/p18407797]Post 430[/url] are basically entirely contribution-based, but you seem to already know that it’s not that reliable. And there really aren’t that many options for other D1 reads you can make; stuff like apathy, motivation, and tone are hard early on in the game. So since I think you recognize the limits of your reads, sorta, I’ll give you something else to think about: 

[hide=Feedback previously given to someone else that I think is relevant.]I think at this point, you have a pretty solid grasp on gameplay and fundamentals, as well as being able to tell whether something is a somewhat stupid idea or not. The next step, here, is being more proactive. There are multiple ways to do this - one of them being more reads, of course - but one thing you can work on is pressure. For instance, is someone lurking? Is someone posting questionable stuff, messing up your reads on them? Then something you can do here is actively go after them. Ask them questions. Ask them about their thoughts, their reads. Ask them to stop trolling. Vote them. Go through their posts, look for common scumtells, and interrogate them over why they did those things.

If you do this, one of three things will happen: 1, you will gain a better understanding of their thought process, developing a more accurate read on them even if their posts look questionable at first glance; 2, they will buckle under the pressure you are putting them under, potentially freezing up and becoming even more scummy; or, 3, they will actually start paying attention to the game, contributing, and being active.

All of these three things are amazing for town and can turn around a game, so applying pressure is definitely something you can think about in your future games. Of course, you have to make sure you aren’t overly tunneling or confirmation-biasing, and you should stop to reconsider if you feel like you’ve gone too deep. And if you end up pressuring a PR, there’s a chance that won’t end up well either. However, on the whole, being proactive is definitely better than watching people mess up and troll and just doom the game.

Best of luck in your future games![/hide]

Like, the way you [url=https://aops.com/community/p18413077]questioned MaverickMan[/url] was great. But I think that happened because MaverickMan wasn’t happy with your read on him? Proactively going after people like this, questioning them and applying pressure, can be more useful than trying to form passive reads. You’ll get a better grasp on their playstyle (hopefully), and or maybe pressure them enough for them to crack.
